core_children.core_content: The Upkeep Rebate Primitive is by default Globally Activated in all Scaffold Agent Artifacts; it is not possible to deactivate it.

core_children.core_name: Prohibition On Deactivating Both Distribution Requirement And Market Cap Fee Primitives
core_children.core_content: An Agent must always have either the Distribution Requirement Primitive or the Market Cap Fee Primitive Globally Activated; it is not possible to deactivate both.

An Agent may reactivate a Globally Inactive Primitive according to the governance process defined in its Root Edit Primitive. Reactivating the Primitive restores the Agent’s ability to create new instances (via proper Invocation) but does not automatically revive previously deactivated instances. Each existing instance remains inactive unless separately reactivated through the appropriate governance process.

section_primary_docs.core_content: The documents herein define Primitive Instance Status.

core_children.core_content: Each valid Invocation of a Primitive causes the Agent Artifact to be updated with a specific instance of a Primitive. For example, each Invocation of the Token SkyLink Primitive launches a SkyLink deployment to a specific blockchain. The Agent may manage each Primitive instance independently (e.g., Activate or suspend it as circumstances change). A Primitive instance thus has its own Status or life cycle that is independent of the Primitive’s Global Activation Status.
The documents herein specify the potential values of the Status of an instance of a Primitive. An instance of a Primitive must always have exactly one of these values.

The instance Status of [code]Active[/code] indicates that an instance of a Primitive is fully operational and may be used for its intended purpose by the Agent and potentially other parties. For example, a Token SkyLink deployment is active and can be used to bridge tokens between blockchains.

The instance Status of [code]Suspended[/code] indicates that an instance of a Primitive was [code]Active[/code] at one point in time and may be [code]Active[/code] again, but is not currently operational. This may be due to the Primitive not meeting performance expectations (e.g. for an Allocation System instance), security issues, or due to failure of the Agent operating the Primitive to satisfy other requirements such as those specified in the Risk Capital or Asset Liability Management frameworks.

The instance Status of [code]Completed[/code] indicates that an Instance of a Primitive has reached a terminal state and will not become [code]Active[/code] again. This status applies in two cases: 1) a previously active Instance has permanently ceased operations; or 2) an Instance designed for a single Invocation has achieved its intended outcome and requires no further management (e.g., the one-time deployment of the Star Transformation Primitive).

A Star Agent that has a Globally Active Primitive may freely create (assuming the Primitive was properly Invoked), suspend, archive, or update individual instances of that Primitive, subject to the rules defined in the Agent Artifact and the Sky Core Atlas. For example, the Agent may run multiple Integration Boost instances in parallel (each with its own markets and configurations), toggling them on or off as needed, without affecting the underlying Integration Boost Primitive’s Global Activation Status. Changing a Primitive’s Instance-Level Status can be effected through the governance process defined in the Root Edit Primitive, or through an appropriately configured Omni Document. See A.1.13.2.7.2 - Omni Document Process.

section_primary_docs.core_content: The documents herein define the process by which an Agent may Invoke instances of Sky Primitives. If the Invocation is valid, the Agent Artifact is upgraded and the Agent gains the Primitives’ specific functionality.

core_children.core_content: The documents herein define the Statuses assigned to an Invocation that is in progress.

To Invoke a Primitive, an Agent must supply all required inputs as defined in the Sky Core Atlas. Every Primitive instance abides by a standardized data model specified at the Sky Core level, ensuring that all Star Agents, Executor Agents and other actors are able to submit and track the necessary data reliably.

The actual submission and handling of these data inputs occurs exclusively through the Powerhouse interface, which serves as the Sky ecosystem’s shared data infrastructure. Powerhouse thus becomes the canonical gateway for exchanging information with the Primitive, enforcing consistency in both the format of the data and the steps taken to Invoke (and later update) the Primitive.

Once the Agent has provided all required inputs to the relevant Primitive(s), the proposed Artifact Update undergoes review / validation by designated actors such as Operational Executor Facilitators. The identity and responsibilities of these actors are detailed in subsequent documents of this Article.
After the Primitive inputs have been validated, Agent token holders vote on whether to upgrade the Artifact with the Primitive(s).

If the vote is successful, the Agent Artifact is officially upgraded with a Primitive Instance, which latter has its own independent Status that is distinct from the Primitive’s Global Activation Status. See A.2.3.1.3 -  Primitive Instance Status. This upgrade means that the respective Primitive(s) is formally integrated into the Agent’s Artifact and the Atlas as a whole; the Star Agent can now operationalize the Primitive’s special functionality.

section_primary_docs.core_content: The documents herein list the current Sky Primitives and set forth the process for amending them. Each Primitive is defined in more detail below.

core_children.core_content:
The current Sky Primitives are:

• Genesis Primitives
   ◦ Agent Creation Primitive
   ◦ Star Transformation Primitive
   ◦ Executor Transformation Primitive
   ◦ Agent Token Primitive

• Operational Primitives
   ◦ Executor Accord Primitive
   ◦ Root Edit Primitive
   ◦ Light Agent Primitive

• Ecosystem Upkeep Primitives
   ◦ Distribution Requirement Primitive
   ◦ Market Cap Fee Primitive
   ◦ Upkeep Rebate Primitive

• SkyLink Primitives
   ◦ Token SkyLink Primitive

• Demand Side Stablecoin Primitives
   ◦ Accessibility Reward Primitive
   ◦ Integration Boost Primitive
   ◦ Pioneer Chain Primitive

• Supply Side Stablecoin Primitives
   ◦ Allocation System Primitive
   ◦ Junior Risk Capital Rental Primitive
   ◦ Actively Stabilizing Collateral Rental Primitive

• Core Governance Primitives
   ◦ Core Governance Reward Primitive
The set of available Sky Primitives may be amended in accordance with the governance processes established in the Sky Core Atlas.

article_sections.section_content:
Process Definitions function as the first-class objects through which Sky Primitives transform high-level governance logic into actionable document-driven workflows. All Sky Primitive Process Definitions are structured according to a common data schema - a set of fields that describe when they can start, how they run step by step, and what they must input or produce upon completion.

The documents herein define this data schema for Sky Primitive Process Definitions, which are applied to the universal specifications for each Sky Primitive. This common data schema aligns the entire ecosystem, while each Agent Artifact automatically references and extends the universal rules to incorporate the Agent’s unique Instance-level strategies, parameters and document-driven process flows.

At present, only the Accessibility Reward Primitive and the Integration Boost Primitive specifications are structured using this schema. In future iterations of the Atlas, the schema will be applied to all Primitive specifications in this Article.

section_primary_docs.core_content: The documents herein define when a Process is triggered, and what conditions must be satisfied, if any, for the Process to properly initiate.

core_children.core_content: The presence of a Time-Based Trigger or Document Update Trigger for a given Process does not necessarily mean such Trigger is the sole means to initiate the Process. For example, an Atlas Document that is external to the Sky Primitives specifications may authorize a Facilitator to manually initiate a process.
This field defines the triggering date/time for recurring or scheduled Processes.

Document Update Triggers are a type of gating condition that automatically or deterministically compels initiation of a Process in response to a specific Core Atlas or Agent Artifact Document Update in a previous Process Definition.

Once the specified Atlas or Artifact Document Update occurs (and assuming any specified Dependencies are also met), the Process must be initiated.

If no such deterministic Document Update-based trigger exists for a Process to initiate, this field is set to “None.”

Dependencies are gating conditions that must be satisfied for the Process to be able to proceed, regardless of a triggering event.

Examples of Dependencies include actors possessing suitable permissions, or a key stakeholder consenting to move forward.

If a trigger has occurred, but Dependencies are unmet, the Process cannot proceed.

section_primary_docs.core_content: This field describes the step-by-step operational procedure that occurs once a Process is properly initiated. A step can reference specific Core Atlas or Artifact Documents and broadly describe how their state is manipulated and decisions are made. More granular specifications regarding Document state changes are defined in the [code]Required Primitive Inputs[/code] schema component A.2.3.2.3 -  Required Primitive Inputs. Process flow steps can both consume and produce relevant Document data.

This field specifies the Agent Artifact Documents and their associated fields that must be manipulated using the Powerhouse interface as part of a Process. If any of the required Inputs is missing or incorrect, the Process cannot be completed.

core_children.core_content:
Some Processes require Primitive Inputs that are organized into multiple sequential stages (also called “Input stages”). Each Input stage must be completed before progressing to the next, ensuring that all dependencies and validations are met in order. Once an Input stage is completed, the subsequent Input stage is initiated automatically, continuing in this manner until all stages have been finished. 

Upon completing the final Primitive Input stage, the Process Definition transitions to the [code]Required Outputs[/code] schema component; this component defines the necessary updates to Sky Core Atlas or Agent Artifact Documents.

Where a Process Definition has more than one set of [code]Required Outputs[/code], with each set corresponding to (or “triggered by”) a particular Input stage, the [code]Required Primitive Inputs[/code] schema component must explicitly correlate each Input stage with the [code]Required Outputs[/code] it triggers. See A.2.3.2.4.1 -  Multiple Required Outputs And Their Respective Input Stage or Mutually Exclusive Pathway.

Some Processes require Primitive Inputs that are organized into two or more mutually exclusive pathways (also “mutually exclusive Input pathways”). Once a pathway is chosen—either manually or automatically via the application of a defined decision or condition—the Process follows that pathway through to completion, rendering the other pathways inapplicable.

When multiple “sets” of [code]Required Outputs[/code] exist, with each set corresponding to (or “triggered by”) a particular Mutually Exclusive Input Pathway, the [code]Required Primitive Inputs[/code] schema component must explicitly correlate each Mutually Exclusive Input Pathway with the [code]Required Outputs[/code] it triggers. Because the pathways are mutually exclusive, only the Required Output set associated with the selected Pathway will be executed. The other Output sets remain inactive.

section_primary_docs.core_content:
This field specifies the particular Sky Core Atlas and/or Agent Artifact Documents that must be updated as an end result of a Process.

Where applicable, a Document Update can serve as a “trigger” that deterministically compels another Process Definition to be initiated by a system or actor. The [code]Trigger - Process[/code] field links to the respective Process Definition that is triggered by the Required Output.

Some processes do not need formal “Required Outputs” because the only process flow step is a simple update of an Artifact Document. In these edge cases, the change specified in [code]Required Primitive Inputs[/code] (e.g., toggling a field to Globally Activate a Primitive) fully completes the process, effectively completing it in a single step. Once the designated Document and field are updated as prescribed, the Process is considered finalized.

core_children.core_name: Multiple Required Outputs And Their Respective Input Stage or Mutually Exclusive Pathway
core_children.core_content:
Where a Process Definition has more than one set of Required Outputs, and each set corresponds to (or is “triggered by”) either a specific Input stage or a specific Mutually Exclusive Input Pathway, the Process Definition’s [code]Required Primitive Input[/code] field must define which [code]Required Output[/code] set is Invoked upon completion of that stage or pathway.

1. For Sequential Stages
When multiple sequential stages exist, each stage’s successful completion triggers its corresponding Required Output set. As subsequent stages are completed in turn, each triggers its own distinct Required Outputs, ensuring that all designated outputs eventually execute in sequence.

2. For Mutually Exclusive Pathways
If the Required Primitive Input process instead (or additionally) involves mutually exclusive paths, once a pathway is chosen—either manually or automatically by a specified condition—only the Required Output set tied to that pathway is applied. Outputs associated with the unselected/unexecuted pathways remain inactive.

In the Sky Primitives Data Schema, references to an Agent Artifact Document are made in terms of the generic Document type. See A.1.2.2.2 - List Of Document Types And Their Specifications. In practice, each Agent has its own Instance of that Document type in its Artifact. Thus, when a Primitive’s process flow indicates that an Agent Artifact Document must be updated, it is to be interpreted as referring to the specific Instance of that Document type in the Star Agent’s Artifact.

article_sections.section_name: Prerequisites For Activating Agent Creation Primitive
article_sections.section_content: The Agent Creation Primitive is the first Sky Primitive that must be activated by prospective Agent founders. Because an Agent Artifact does not yet exist at this stage, the prerequisite requirements set forth herein must be met, including off-chain obligations and governance outputs. Only after satisfying these prerequisites does the Agent Creation Primitive become accessible to the prospective founder.

section_primary_docs.core_content: The prospective Agent founder must deploy the required startup capital and pay the Agent creation fee.

core_children.core_content: The required capital and the process for deploying it will be specified in a future iteration of the Atlas.
The prospective founder must pay the required creation fee. The required fee and the process for paying it will be specified in a future iteration of the Atlas.

section_primary_docs.core_content: After the prospective Agent founder deploys the required startup capital and pays the Agent creation fee, Core GovOps creates a Proto-Agent and sets up a Scaffold Agent Artifact.

core_children.core_content: Core GovOps creates a Proto-Agent with no specific functionality in the ecosystem.

Core GovOps prepares a Scaffold Agent Artifact  (”Scaffold Artifact”) containing all Sky Primitives. In the Scaffold Artifact, the Upkeep Rebate Primitive is globally activated by default. See A.2.3.1.2.2 -  Initial Primitive Global Activation Status. All other Primitives are initially set to [code]Inactive[/code]. The Scaffold Artifact also includes an initial set of Omni Documents that provide general information about the Agent and organize the various Sky Primitive Instance Configuration Documents. Core GovOps must add the Scaffold Artifact to the Atlas’ Agent Artifact Scope.
The address deploying the start-up capital is assigned [code]Founder Access[/code] to the Scaffold Agent Artifact. See A.2.3.1.1.3.2 -  Founder Access.

article_sections.section_content: Genesis Primitives are a category of Primitives addressing different aspects of initial Agent setup—such as Agent creation, token launch and configuration, and transformation pathways.

section_primary_docs.core_content: This Primitive may only be Invoked after the prospective Agent founder meets the prerequisites defined in A.2.3.3 -  Prerequisites For Activating Agent Creation Primitive, resulting in the creation of a Proto-Agent. Invoking the Agent Creation Primitive allows the Proto-Agent to establish its identity by declaring its name, as well as articulate its intended vision, business model or ecosystem goals.

core_children.core_content: The documents herein define the Process Definition for initial setup and ongoing management of an Instance of the Agent Creation Primitive.
The documents herein define the process for setting up an Instance of the Agent Creation Primitive.
The Founder uses the Powerhouse interface to input the Agent's name and an introduction outlining the Agent's vision.

Core GovOps validates the Founder’s inputs. This includes verifying that all of the documents created by the Founder using Founder Access are well-specified, that the documents are Aligned, and that all necessary Primitives to complete setup have been Activated. The necessary Primitives are the Agent Creation, Star/Executor Transformation, Agent Token, Executor Accord, and Root Edit Primitives. In addition, at least one of the Distribution Requirement or the Market Cap Fee Primitives must be Activated. (See A.2.3.1.1.3.1 -  Founder Required Primitive Activation.) After confirming these conditions, Core GovOps creates a Genesis Account and a SubProxy Account for the Agent.

After successful validation, the Agent Creation Primitive is considered successfully Invoked, and the Agent Artifact is officially upgraded to reflect an Agent Creation Primitive Instance, with a Status of [code]Completed[/code].
The documents herein define the process for the ongoing management of an Instance of the Agent Creation Primitive.

core_children.core_name: Agent Creation Primitive Results In One-Time Creation
core_children.core_content: Because the Agent Creation Primitive is deployed solely to effect the one-time creation of the Agent, no further management process is needed post-deployment.
The documents herein define the required inputs for a valid Invocation of the Agent Creation Primitive. If any input is noncompliant or omitted, the Primitive will be invalidated.
The Agent Creation Primitive must be Globally Activated.
The Agent Creation Primitive must specify the Agent’s Name and provide a brief overview of its vision or business model.

The Agent Creation Primitive must specify the SubProxy address of the Agent. This field is populated by Core GovOps. The SubProxy is an account that serves as the Agent’s treasury. The SubProxy Account of a Star Agent is operationally controlled by the Operational Executor Agent specified in the Executor Accord Primitive. The SubProxy Account of an Operational Executor Agent will be operationally controlled according to a process specified in a future iteration of the Agent Creation Primitive.

The Agent Creation Primitive must designate the Genesis Account.  This field is populated by Core GovOps. The Genesis account initially controls 100% of the tokens of the Agent. The Genesis Account is initially controlled by the Agent Founder.

section_primary_docs.core_content: Prior to Activating this Primitive, a Proto-Agent has been created, meaning it has not yet adopted any specialized role. Since a Proto-Agent cannot perform actions in the Sky Ecosystem, it must first transform into either a Star Agent or Executor Agent to gain functionality. The Star Transformation Primitive defined herein allows an Agent to transform into a Star Agent, subject to certain conditions.

core_children.core_content: The documents herein define the Process Definition for initial setup and ongoing management of an Instance of the Star Transformation Primitive.
The documents herein define the process for setting up the Star Transformation Primitive.

The Proto-Agent must use the Powerhouse interface to input their desired Agent Type into the Primitive. For the Star Transformation Primitive process, the Proto-Agent must specify ‘Star Agent’ as their desired Agent Type.

Core GovOps validates the Proto-Agent’s inputs, namely, the Agent Type.  Additionally, Core GovOps performs a further review to confirm that all the documents created by the Founder using Founder Access are well-specified, that the documents are Aligned, and that all necessary Primitives have been Activated. The necessary Primitives are the Agent Creation, Star/Executor Transformation, Agent Token, Executor Accord, and Root Edit Primitives. In addition, at least one of the Distribution Requirement or the Market Cap Fee Primitives must be Activated. (See A.2.3.1.1.3.1 -  Founder Required Primitive Activation.)

After successful validation, the Star Transformation Primitive is considered successfully Invoked, and the Agent Artifact is officially upgraded to reflect a Star Transformation Primitive Instance, with a Status of [code]Completed[/code].
The documents herein define the process for the ongoing management of an Instance of the Star Transformation Primitive.

core_children.core_name: Star Transformation Primitive Results In One-Time Creation
core_children.core_content: Because the Star Transformation Primitive is deployed solely to effect the one-time transformation of the Proto-Agent, no further management process is needed post-deployment.
The documents herein define the required inputs for a valid Invocation of the Star Transformation Primitive.  If any input is noncompliant or omitted, the Primitive will be invalidated.
The Star Transformation Primitive must be Globally Activated.
The Star Transformation Primitive must specify the Star Agent’s Type, e.g., that it is a Star Agent.

section_primary_docs.core_content: The Executor Transformation Primitive allows an Agent to transform into an Executor Agent, subject to certain conditions.
The Agent Token Primitive enables Agents to define, mint, and distribute their governance tokens including foundation allocations, token rewards, and airdrops.

core_children.core_content: The documents herein define the Process Definition for initial setup and ongoing management of an Instance of the Agent Token Primitive.
The documents herein define the process for setting up the Agent Token Primitive.

The Agent must use the Powerhouse interface to input key data into the Agent Token Primitive, including token name, ticker, symbol, genesis supply, total supply, distribution rules, emissions schedule, pending token address and token admin address. The token admin address should be the Agent SubProxy Account. The Agent must also specify whether token emissions beyond the current supply have been irreversibly disabled.
Core GovOps validates the Agent’s inputs.

After successful validation, the Agent Token Primitive is considered successfully Invoked. The Agent Artifact is officially upgraded to reflect an Agent Token Primitive Instance, with a Status of [code]Active[/code].

Upon successful validation, the token contract can now be deployed on-chain by Core GovOps. The minted supply must be allocated as specified in the Agent Token Primitive Instance. The Primitive is automatically updated to replace the pending token address with the actual token address.
The documents herein define the process for the ongoing management of an Instance of the Agent Token Primitive.

core_children.core_name: Agent Token Primitive Results In One-Time Creation
core_children.core_content: Because the Agent Token Primitive is deployed solely to create a one-off Token for an Agent, no further management process is needed post-deployment.
The documents herein define the required inputs for a valid Invocation of the Agent Token Primitive. If any input is noncompliant or omitted, the Primitive will be invalidated.
The Agent Token Primitive must be Globally Activated.
The Agent Token Primitive must specify the name of the token.
The Agent Token Primitive must specify the symbol of the token.
The Agent Token Primitive must specify the genesis supply of the token.
The Agent Token Primitive must specify either the pending or permanent address of the token (depending on whether the Primitive has been successfully Invoked).
The Agent Token Primitive must specify the Admin of the token.

The Agent Token Primitive must specify whether token emissions beyond the current supply have been irreversibly disabled. Once Disabled is set to [code]True[/code], the action cannot be undone by the Agent. Sky Governance retains the ability to revert the Disabled setting where the Agent is in violation of Risk Capital requirements and emissions are required by the Risk Framework.
The Agent Token Primitive must specify the process for distributing the initial token supply.

article_sections.section_content: Operational Primitives are a category of Primitives enabling the Agents’ own operation and governance and AI features, as well as the ability to create derivative Light Agents.

section_primary_docs.core_content: The Executor Accord Primitive is the foundational mechanism that allows Star Agents to operate autonomously according to the strategy specified in their Agent Artifacts with automated operational insurance provided by separate Operational Executor Agents that delegate the work to GovOps actors.

core_children.core_content: The documents herein define the Process Definition for initial setup and ongoing management of an Instance of the Executor Accord Primitive.
The documents herein define the process for setting up an Instance of the Executor Accord Primitive.

The Star Agent and Operational Executor Agent must come to a consensus about the details of the Executor Accord. These details must be entered into the Powerhouse interface along with independent confirmation from each Agent that they agree to those terms.
Core GovOps validates the Agent’s inputs, ensuring that the terms of the Executor Accord are reasonably specific.

After successful validation, the Executor Accord Primitive is considered successfully Invoked. The Agent Artifact is officially upgraded to reflect an Executor Accord Primitive Instance, with a Status of [code]Active[/code].

core_children.core_name: Operational GovOps Takes Over Operational Duties
core_children.core_content: Upon successful validation, the Star Agent has a documented relationship with an Executor Agent and so Core GovOps will no longer perform validation of the Agent’s Primitive inputs. Instead, the Operational GovOps associated with the Executor Agent specified in the Executor Accord will carry out operational tasks on behalf of the Star Agent.
The documents herein define the process for the ongoing management of an Instance of the Executor Accord Primitive.

The documents herein define the required inputs for a valid Invocation of the Executor Accord Primitive. If any input is noncompliant or omitted, the Primitive will be invalidated and the Executor Accord will not be set up.
The Executor Accord Primitive must be Globally Activated.
The Executor Accord Primitive must include the terms of the Executor Accord between the Star Agent and Operational Executor Agent.
The Executor Accord Primitive must include independent confirmation from each Agent that they agree to the terms of the Executor Accord.

section_primary_docs.core_content: The Root Edit Primitive allows Star Agents, through a token holder vote, to direct the Operational Executor Agent specified in the Executor Accord Primitive to directly modify the Star Agent Artifact.

core_children.core_content: The documents herein define the Process Definition for initial setup and ongoing management of an Instance of the Root Edit Primitive.
The documents herein define the process for setting up an Instance of the Root Edit Primitive.
The Agent must use the Powerhouse interface to specify the process by which Root Edits occur. See A.1.13.2.7 - Artifact Edit Processes.
Core GovOps validates the Agent’s inputs.

After successful validation, the Root Edit Primitive is considered successfully Invoked. The Agent Artifact is officially upgraded to reflect a Root Edit Primitive Instance, with a Status of [code]Active[/code].
The documents herein define the process for the ongoing management of an Instance of the Root Edit Primitive.

core_children.core_name: Root Edit Primitive Artifact Edit Proposal
core_children.core_content: The process for using the Root Edit Primitive begins with a party presenting a proposal for an Artifact Edit. The Root Edit Primitive specifies the requirements to submit a proposal, the required form of the proposal, and any other prerequisites that must be satisfied prior to the proposal being voted on.

core_children.core_name: Root Edit Primitive Review By Operational Facilitator
core_children.core_content: The Operational Facilitator reviews the proposal. This review encompasses two aspects. First, the Operational Executor reviews the proposal for alignment with the Atlas. Second, the Facilitator reviews the proposal for compliance with any requirements set out in the Root Edit Primitive for the Agent, such as eligible actors to submit proposals, form of the proposal, or required time for review before submitting a proposal.

If the Operational Facilitator concludes that the proposal is aligned with the Atlas and consistent with the process specified in the Root Edit Primitive, then the Operational Facilitator moves forward with conducting the vote. The Root Edit Primitive specifies how the vote should be conducted (e.g. on-chain or off-chain), the time period over which voting should occur, and other relevant parameters for the vote such as quorum and approval requirements.

After the voting period ends, the Operational Facilitator reviews the outcome of the vote. If the vote is successful, the Operational Facilitator actions the Artifact Edit in the Powerhouse system. In either case, the Operational Facilitator records the outcome of the vote, including all pertinent materials, in the Powerhouse system.

The documents herein define the required inputs for a valid Invocation of the Root Edit Primitive. If any input is noncompliant or omitted, the Primitive will be invalidated and the Root Edit Primitive will not be set up.
The Root Edit Primitive must be Globally Activated.

The Root Edit Primitive must specify the process by which updates to the Agent Artifact may be made by Agent token holder vote. The details of this process may be specified by the Agent, subject to the following conditions: (1) Agent token holders must vote to approve Artifact Edit proposals, (2) the Operational Facilitator must review each proposal for alignment and conformance with the process specified in the Root Edit Primitive, (3) the vote must be conducted by the Operational Facilitator, and (4) the Operational Facilitator must action the Artifact Edit if the vote passes. The process definition must include the elements included in the documents herein.
The Root Edit Primitive must specify the required format of proposals.
The Root Edit Primitive must specify the requirements for actors to be eligible to submit proposals.

core_children.core_name: R equirements For Proposals To Be Included In Vote
core_children.core_content: The Root Edit Primitive must specify the requirements for proposals to be included in a vote, such as any required review period or any reviews that must be conducted by other parties such as expert advisors.
The Root Edit Primitive must specify the period over which voting should occur.
The Root Edit Primitive must specify the percent of outstanding tokens that must participate in a vote for it to be considered valid.
The Root Edit Primitive must specify the percent of tokens participating in the vote that must vote in favor of the proposal for it to be approved.

core_children.core_name: Handling Of Emergency Or Urgent Situations
core_children.core_content: The Root Edit Primitive must specify any procedures for expedited voting in emergency or urgent situations.
The Root Edit Primitive must specify any exceptions to the general process, such as subject matter requiring supermajority approval.

section_primary_docs.core_content: The Light Agent Primitive enables users to create Light Agents, which are sub-agents operating on top of the Agent’s Executor Accord, conferring the advantages of Sky GovOps at a lower cost, but without direct access to other Sky Primitives.

article_sections.section_content: Ecosystem Upkeep Primitives ensure that all Agents contribute to long-term ecosystem sustainability. Star Agents must choose between either the Distribution Requirement Primitive or the Market Cap Fee Primitive; whereas the Upkeep Rebate Primitive applies universally to all Star Agents. The term “Ecosystem Upkeep Fees” refers to the upkeep obligation of a Star Agent pursuant to either the Distribution Requirement Primitive or the Market Cap Fee Primitive.

section_primary_docs.core_content:
The Distribution Requirement Primitive requires Agents to periodically buy and distribute 0.25% of their token supply per year. The annual requirement of 0.25% of the token supply is divided into twelve (12) equal monthly distributions. Distributions must occur on the first day of each month, with tokens purchased by the Star Agent on the open market and transferred to an address designated by Sky Core in A.2.3.6.1.1 -  Sky Core-Designated Address. 

Star Agents choose between fulfilling either the Distribution Requirement Primitive or the Market Cap Fee Primitive.

core_children.core_content: The address to which tokens must be transferred will be specified in a future iteration of the Atlas.

The value of the distributed tokens is calculated as the number of tokens distributed, multiplied by the Time-Weighted Average Price (TWAP) of the token over the 24-hour period ending at 23:59 UTC on the last day of each month, immediately preceding the distribution event. This value is applied in the rebate calculation under the Upkeep Rebate Primitive. See A.2.3.6.3 -  Upkeep Rebate Primitive.

section_primary_docs.core_content:
The Market Cap Fee Primitive requires Star Agents to pay an annual fee equivalent to 0.30% of their market capitalization to Sky, accounted for monthly. The annual fee of 0.30% of the market capitalization is divided into twelve (12) equal monthly payments, equating to 0.025% of the market capitalization per month. Payments must occur on the first day of each month, with the fee transferred to an address designated by Sky Core in A.2.3.6.2.1 -  Sky Core-Designated Address. 

Star Agents choose between fulfilling either the Distribution Requirement Primitive or the Market Cap Fee Primitive.

core_children.core_content: The address to which the fee must be transferred will be specified in a future iteration of the Atlas.

The market capitalization is determined as the total token supply multiplied by the TWAP of the token over the 24-hour period ending at 23:59 UTC on the last day of each month, immediately preceding the payment event. This value is applied in the rebate calculation under the Upkeep Rebate Primitive. See A.2.3.6.3 -  Upkeep Rebate Primitive.

section_primary_docs.core_content:
The Upkeep Rebate Primitive allows a Star Agent (”Holding Agent”) to claim a rebate on its Ecosystem Upkeep Fees when it holds any portion of the token supply of another Star Agent (”Issuing Agent”).

Ecosystem Upkeep Fees are accounted on a monthly basis. The Upkeep Rebate is calculated on the same cadence per Holding Agent as follows:

(1) The Holding Agent’s share of the Issuing Agent’s token supply at the time the Issuing Agent pays its Ecosystem Upkeep Fees, multiplied by
(2) The total monthly Ecosystem Upkeep Fees paid by the Issuing Agent. For a Star Agent deploying the Distribution Requirement Primitive, the value of its total monthly Ecosystem Upkeep Fee is calculated pursuant to A.2.3.6.1.2 -  Calculation of Distributed Tokens. For a Star Agent deploying the Market Cap Fee Primitive, the value of its total monthly Ecosystem Upkeep Fee is calculated pursuant to  A.2.3.6.2.2 -  Valuation.

This resulting rebate amount is applied against the Holding Agent’s Ecosystem Upkeep Fees due in the calendar month immediately following the Issuing Agent’s payment.

article_sections.section_content: SkyLink Primitives are Sky Primitives that are technical infrastructure that extends the Sky Protocol. SkyLink Primitives are built autonomously by Star Agents, but owned by Sky Core and shared among all Star Agents. Star Agents are reimbursed for the cost of setting up SkyLink Primitives and given additional first-mover incentives.

section_primary_docs.core_content: The Token SkyLink Primitive allows users to bridge USDS, sUSDS, SKY, or an Agent token to new blockchains and enables other multichain features.

core_children.core_content: The documents herein define the Process Definition for initial setup and ongoing management of the Token SkyLink Primitive.
The documents herein define the process for setting up an Instance of the Token SkyLink Primitive.

core_children.core_name: Token SkyLink Setup Real World Agreements And Planning
core_children.core_content: The documents herein define the preliminary, off-chain human coordination stage of setting up an Instance of the Token SkyLink Primitive.

core_children.core_name: Token SkyLink Setup Target Chain Identification And Feasibility Analysis
core_children.core_content: The Star Agent researches potential target blockchains for token bridging, including evaluation of user base, DeFi ecosystem, and bridging security requirements. The Star Agent confirms that the target chain supports LayerZero or can be integrated easily with the LayerZero Omnichain Fungible Token standard. The Star Agent also estimates potential use of the token on the target chain to justify the bridge deployment and audit costs. The output of this step is a preliminary decision to proceed with deploying a bridge to the target chain including a scope of work for the bridge deployment and estimated audit costs.

core_children.core_name: Token SkyLink Setup Initial Alignment With Operational GovOps
core_children.core_content: The Star Agent presents the bridging plan to Operational GovOps, including a technical summary, proposed timeline, and estimate of audit costs. The Star Agent and Operational GovOps also discuss any guidelines in the Atlas or the Agent Artifact that may affect bridging. The Star Agent receives early feedback on the plan and modifies it accordingly. The output of this step is an informal agreement to proceed with developing a bridge deployment along with any documented conditions or feedback from Operational GovOps.

core_children.core_name: Token SkyLink Setup Audit Preparation And Proposal Of Costs
core_children.core_content: The Star Agent contacts a reputable third-party security audit firm for reviewing the bridge implementation. The Star Agent negotiates audit scope, timeline, and fees with the audit firm. The Star Agent documents the projected cost for the bridge, including development, audit, and deployment costs, for future reimbursement. The outcome of this step is a formal agreement with the audit firm and a detailed cost breakdown for bridge deployment and auditing.

core_children.core_name: Token SkyLink Setup Codification and Validation
core_children.core_content: The documents herein define how agreements to setup an Instance of the Token SkyLink Primitive are codified and validated in the Powerhouse system and how governance votes happen.

The Star Agent drafts an update to the Star Agent Artifact adding the Token SkyLink to the list of active Token SkyLink deployments and including the information specified in A.2.3.7.1.2.2 -  List of Active Token SkyLink Deployments. The Star Agent submits the draft to the Powerhouse system. The output of this step is a draft Star Agent Artifact update in the Powerhouse system.

The Operational Executor Facilitator reviews the proposal to ensure that it is complete and aligned with the Atlas. The Operational Executor Facilitator then initiates an off-chain snapshot vote, following the quorum and majority rules in the Star Agent Artifact. When complete, the result of the vote is recorded in the Powerhouse system. The output of this step is the snapshot vote result recorded in the Powerhouse system.

If the Token SkyLink Invocation is successfully approved, the Operational Executor Facilitator finalizes and publishes the update to the Star Agent Artifact, making it effective in the Atlas. The output of this step is an updated Star Agent Artifact with the Token SkyLink Instance Activated.
The documents herein define how the deployment of an Instance of the Token SkyLink Primitive is executed on-chain.

core_children.core_name: Token SkyLink Setup Bridge Deployment And Initial Audit
core_children.core_content: The Star Agent, through Operational GovOps, deploys the Token SkyLink contract on the target chain. The Star Agent shares the Token SkyLink contract’s final address with the audit firm for verification. Operational GovOps confirms that the bridge contract address and references match the updated Star Agent Artifact. The audit firm completes and updates the bridging audit registry with the results of the audit. The output of this step is a deployed bridge contract with audit results uploaded to the bridging audit registry.

core_children.core_name: Token SkyLink Setup Activation On New Chain
core_children.core_content: Core GovOps formalizes the audit results with a Sky Core Executive Vote to fully activate bridging functionality. Operational GovOps updates the Star Agent Reimbursement Module to begin counting any bridged tokens toward the incremental reward. The output of this step is fully enabled bridging on the target chain with the Star Agent Reimbursement Module updated to track bridging based rewards.
The documents herein define the process for managing an Instance of the Token SkyLink Primitive.

During each settlement cycle, the Star Agent Reimbursement Module tallies the total token bridging volume on the new chain. The Star Agent Reimbursement Module calculates the reimbursement due for bridge development costs as a percent of the token bridging volume, in addition to normal Accessibility Rewards if applicable. Core GovOps executes a payment to the Star Agent for the amount due for reimbursement of bridge development costs, to the extent that such costs have not already been fully reimbursed. Core GovOps updates the Powerhouse system to reflect the amount of bridge development costs that have been reimbursed.

The documents herein define the required inputs for a valid Token SkyLink Primitive. If any input is noncompliant or omitted, the Primitive will be invalidated and the SkyLink deployment will not move forward.
The Token SkyLink Primitive must be Globally Activated.

core_children.core_name: List of Active Token SkyLink Deployments
core_children.core_content: The Token SkyLink Primitive must list each active Token SkyLink deployment. The listing must include the following information: (1), the token being bridged, (2) the target chain of the bridge, (3) the address of the bridge contract on the Ethereum Mainnet, (4) the address of the bridge contract on the target chain, (5) the audit of the bridge contract, (6) the bridge parameters, (7) the total bridge deployment and audit costs, and (8) the amount of bridge deployment and audit costs that have been reimbursed to date.

article_sections.section_content: Demand Side Stablecoin Primitives are Sky Primitives that target demand generation for USDS. Star Agents can create and control multiple instances of these Primitives which exist as technical infrastructure incentivizing the adoption or usage of USDS by end users or third parties.

section_primary_docs.core_content: The documents herein govern the Accessibility Reward Primitive.

core_children.core_content: The documents herein provide an introduction to the Accessibility Reward Primitive.

The purpose of the Accessibility Reward is to incentivize Star Agents and third parties to drive USDS adoption by providing a financial reward to these actors for all USDS and sUSDS balances attributable to them.
Multiple instances of the Accessibility Reward Primitive are allowed. Each instance corresponds to an Accessibility Reward program with an associated Accessibility Reward Code.

An Agent Artifact that has more than one active instance of the Accessibility Reward Primitive is not required to have a [code]Multi-Instance Coordinator Document[/code], since each Instance can be managed independently.

The requirements herein apply universally across all possible deployments of the Accessibility Reward Primitive by Star Agents. They include the steps that Agents must take to deploy the Primitive, including Global Activation of the Primitive, Instance Invocation, and ongoing management of the Primitive Instance(s).
The documents herein define base elements of the Accessibility Reward Primitive.

Integrators are actors that offer access to the Sky Protocol via their frontends or infrastructure. The documents herein define the Integrator Program, which includes the Accessibility Reward and Integration Boost. (Base elements specific to the Integration Boost Primitive are defined in A.2.3.8.2.2.1 - Base Elements)
The documents herein define the requirements for Integrators.

The Integrator must be aligned with Sky’s overall strategy regarding promoting adoption of USDS. In the near term, this determination of a prospective or current Integrator’s alignment with Sky’s overall strategy is made by Operational GovOps in consultation with Ecosystem Actor Viridian Advisors. In the future Operational GovOps may make this determination themselves or consult with another actor to do so. Sky Core may choose whether to maintain an Integrator’s Reward Code in its sole and absolute discretion.

core_children.core_name: Compliance With Local Laws And Regulations As A Condition Precedent To Integrators Receiving Accessibility Rewards
core_children.core_content:
This document and its subdocuments define the jurisdictional compliance rules applicable to Integrators that operate user-facing frontends that integrate with, and thus offer access to, the Sky Protocol and receive Accessibility Rewards.

Integrators are solely responsible for complying with all relevant legal and regulatory requirements related to their participation in the Integrator Program. Integrators represent and warrant that their participation and activities under the Integrator Program are and will remain in full compliance with all applicable laws and regulations.

In connection with integrating with, and thereby providing access to, the Sky Protocol, Integrators must operate their frontends and infrastructure in compliance with all relevant legal and regulatory requirements in the jurisdictions applicable to their services. This requires compliance with all relevant legal and regulatory requirements in relation to frontend operations, marketing, and promotions in the jurisdictions where the Integrator provides access to the Sky Protocol through the integration.

An Integrator’s right to participate in the Integrator Program is contingent upon its ongoing compliance with all applicable laws and regulations.

core_children.core_name: Consequence For Integrator Non-Compliance With Local Laws And Regulations
core_children.core_content: Sky Ecosystem Governance, in its absolute and unilateral discretion, retains the right to withhold, revoke, or demand immediate repayment of any and all Accessibility Rewards from any Integrator that is determined, suspected, or alleged to be in violation of the Atlas or any legal, regulatory, or other obligations associated with its integration with, and provision of access to, the Sky Protocol.

If Sky Governance removes an Integrator from the Integrator Program, Operational GovOps must remove the Integrator from the list of Current Integrators in A.2.3.8.1.2.1.5.1 - List Of Current Integrators and deactivate the Instances of the Accessibility Reward and Integration Boost Primitive associated with them.
The documents herein define the process for applying to become an Integrator.

In the near term, applications are made directly to Ecosystem Actor Viridian Advisors. Viridian Advisors must create and maintain a thread on the Sky Forum for Integrator Applications. Viridian Advisors reviews applications and coordinates with Star Agents interested in working with specific applicants. Viridian Advisors issues Reward Codes to approved applicants.

core_children.active_data_controller_content:
The list of Integrator Program applicants is defined as Active Data in A.2.3.8.1.2.1.1.2.1.1 - List Of Integrator Applications.

The Active Data is updated as follows:
• The Responsible Party is Viridian Advisors.
• The Update Process must follow the protocol for ‘Direct Edit’.

core_children.core_content: In the long term, Integrator applications come exclusively through Star Agents. Star Agents may establish whatever processes they deem appropriate to receive inbound requests for potential partnerships.
The documents herein define the process for onboarding new Integrators.

The near term Integrator Onboarding process is as follows. When a Star Agent’s Invocation of the Accessibility Reward Primitive involves an actor who is not yet an approved Integrator, that actor must submit an Integrator Application to Viridian Advisors. Viridian Advisors determines, in consultation with Operational GovOps, whether the Integrator Requirements are met, and if so issues a Reward Code to the applicant. After the Reward Code has been issued, the Invocation of the Primitive may proceed.

In the long term, Operational GovOps will review Integrator applications and determine whether the prospective partner satisfies the Integrator Requirements; if so, Operational GovOps issues a Reward Code. Operational GovOps may contract with Viridian Advisors or another actor to perform the work of reviewing applications and issuing Reward Codes.
The documents herein define base elements related to Reward Codes.
The documents herein define the process for assigning Reward Codes.
In the near term, Reward Codes are assigned by Ecosystem Actor Viridian Advisors.

In the long term, Reward Codes are assigned by Operational GovOps. Operational GovOps may contract with Viridian Advisors or another Ecosystem Actor to perform this work for them, at the discretion of Operational GovOps.
To be eligible for the Accessibility Reward, USDS balances must be “marked” with a Reward Code using the agreed-on Tracking Methodology.

The general Tracking Methodology for Ethereum Mainnet is to specify the Reward Code as a parameter to depositing USDS into the Sky Savings Rate contract or Token Rewards contracts. This on-chain deposit data is then combined with withdrawal data. In the interim, this data is further processed by Ecosystem Actor Viridian Advisors to estimate net deposits associated with the Reward Code on a First In First Out (FIFO) basis. In the long term, Operational Executor Agents will wholly manage this process.

core_children.core_name: Ethereum Mainnet CoW Swap Tracking Methodology
core_children.core_content: The Tracking Methodology for CoW Swap on the Ethereum Mainnet is the same as the general process for the Ethereum Mainnet, with the exception that events on CoW Swap’s decentralized network of solvers are tracked instead.

The Tracking Methodology for Base is to specify the Reward Code as a parameter in calls to the Base PSM contract. Conversions from USDS or USDC to sUSDS are considered “deposits” and net deposits are calculated using an approach similar to that on Ethereum Mainnet.

The Tracking Methodologies specified above are not exclusive. Star Agents and Operational GovOps can develop additional Tracking Methodologies, so long as those methodologies reasonably estimate USDS balances that are attributable to the holder of the Reward Code and there is no possibility that the same USDS balances could be “double counted” for multiple Reward Code holders. Tracking methodologies must be based on either (1) on-chain data or (2) off-chain data that can be independently verified or attested to by a third party.

USDS balances are eligible for an Accessibility Reward for a period of ten (10) years from the date of the event marking the USDS balance with the Reward Code. The date of the marking event is determined based on the Primitive Instance’s specified Tracking Methodology.

In the near term, Ecosystem Actor Viridian Advisors manages the list of Actor Reward Codes. In the future, all current Integrators and onboarding Integrators must be specified in A.2.3.8.1.2.1.5 - Current And Onboarding Integrators so that Star Agents, through their Operational Executor Agents, can onboard new partners themselves without having to go through a single party.
The documents herein define the methodology for calculating the Accessibility Reward due to the reward recipient for USDS and sUSDS balances attributable to them.

core_children.core_name: Fee For USDS and sUSDS Balances
core_children.core_content: The reward recipient is entitled to a 0.1% Accessibility Reward annualized on all USDS and sUSDS balances attributable to them.

In addition to the fee specified above, the Star Agent that Invoked the Accessibility Reward Primitive is entitled to a 0.1% management fee annualized on all USDS and sUSDS balances that use the Reward Code.
The documents herein define base elements of the Accessibility Reward Primitive related to distribution of Accessibility Rewards.
The Accessibility Reward is calculated and distributed on a monthly basis.

The Accessibility Reward payment for each month is equal to 

(1) the average balance over the month, times 
(2) the annual Accessibility Reward Fee specified in A.2.3.8.1.2.1.3 - Fees, divided by 
(3) twelve (12).

The Accessibility Reward Fee specified in A.2.3.8.1.2.1.3 - Fees includes all of the following elements:

1) Fees for USDS and sUSDS Balances specified in A.2.3.8.1.2.1.3.1 - Fee For USDS and sUSDS Balances; and
2) the Star Agent Management Fee specified in A.2.3.8.1.2.1.3.2 - Star Agent Management Fee.
The documents herein define the treasury management process.

In the near term, Viridian Advisors calculates the Accessibility Reward on behalf of the Support Facilitators. The Accessibility Facilitators then pay the Accessibility Reward from the Accessibility Reward Controller Wallet located on the Ethereum Mainnet at [code]0x05f471262d15eeca4059dade070e5bed509a4e73[/code] within seven (7) days of the end of every month. The wallet is funded from the Launch Project and may be topped up at the request of the Accessibility Facilitators, up to 3 million USDS.

In the long term, Operational GovOps calculates the Accessibility Reward for each month. Operational GovOps then pays the Accessibility Reward recipient from its Buffer. Later Sky Core reimburses the Operational Agent Buffer for the amount paid as part of the Settlement Cycle. This minimizes the role of Sky Core in Accessibility Reward payments and emphasizes the primary role of the Operational Executor Agent, acting through Operational GovOps, in implementing the Sky Primitives. The process is specified in further detail in A.2.3.8.1.2.4.1 - Routine Protocol.

If it is discovered that previous Accessibility Reward calculations were made erroneously, underpayments are resolved retroactively. In cases where an Integrator was overpaid, the Star Agent associated with the Integrator must reimburse Sky the overpayment amount and can use future Accessibility Rewards that the Integrator earns to reimburse itself.
The documents herein specify current and onboarding Integrators.

core_children.active_data_controller_content:
Current Integrators are Integrators who have a Reward Code specified in an [code]Active[/code] Instance of the Accessibility Reward Primitive. The list of Current Integrators is defined as Active Data in A.2.3.8.1.2.1.5.1 - List Of Current Integrators.

The Active Data is updated as follows:
• The Responsible Party is Operational GovOps.
• The Update Process must follow the protocol for ‘Direct Edit’.

Onboarding Integrators are actors whose application to the Integrator Program has been approved, but are specified in an Instance of the Accessibility Reward Primitive or Integration Boost Primitive that is “Pending”, or is not [code]Active[/code] yet. The list of Onboarding Integrators is defined as Active Data in A.2.3.8.1.2.1.5.2 - List Of Onboarding Integrators. 

The Active Data is updated as follows:
• The Responsible Party is Operational GovOps.
• The Update Process must follow the protocol for ‘Direct Edit’.

core_children.core_content: An Agent who intends to deploy the Accessibility Reward Primitive must first Globally Activate it.
The process initiation logic is specified herein.
Triggers are specified herein.
None.
None.
See A.2.3.1.2.4.1 -  Agent Launch And Sequence of Primitive Global Activation for constraints on when an Agent can Globally Activate this Primitive.
The Star Agent uses the Powerhouse interface to Globally Activate (toggle on) the Accessibility Reward Primitive.

The following inputs must be submitted into the Primitive using the Powerhouse interface:

• Create [code]Primitive Hub Document[/code]
    ◦ Updated Field: Global Activation Status
        • New Value: set to [code]Activated[/code]
The documents herein specify the required outputs from this process.
No Sky Core Atlas documents are updated as the output of this process.
No Agent Artifact documents are updated as the output of this process. The requirements specified in A.2.3.8.1.2.2.3 - Required Primitive Inputs fully complete the Process.

After fulfilling the requirements for Global Activation, an Agent can Invoke its first Instance of the Accessibility Reward Primitive by following the sequential process specified herein. Subsequent Invocations of the Primitive must also adhere to the same requirements defined below.

core_children.core_name: Process Definition For Initial Opportunity Identification And Planning
core_children.core_content: The documents herein specify the process definition for initial opportunity identification and planning.
The process initiation logic is specified herein.
Triggers are specified herein.
None.
None.
This process is dependent on a Star Agent Globally Activating the Accessibility Reward Primitive pursuant to A.2.3.8.1.2.2 - Global Activation.

The process flow is defined herein.

• The Star Agent identifies an opportunity to drive USDS adoption through an Accessibility Reward to either 1) reward an existing Integrator for driving USDS adoption or 2) incentivize a new actor to onboard as an Integrator to drive USDS adoption.

• Existing Integrators

    ◦ The Star Agent and the third party, if applicable, develop a plan to track USDS utilization attributable to the actor using either on-chain or off-chain data.

• Prospective Integrators

    ◦ Near Term process:
        • The Prospective Integrator must first apply to the Integrator program and be approved by Viridian Advisors per A.2.3.8.1.2.1.1.2.1 - Near Term Process.  Post approval, Viridian Advisors issues a Reward Code to the Integrator.
        • The Star Agent and the third party develop a plan to track USDS utilization attributable to the actor using either on-chain or off-chain data.

    ◦ Long Term process:
        • The Prospective Integrator must first apply to the Integrator program and be approved by Operational GovOps pursuant to A.2.3.8.1.2.1.1.2.2 - Long Term Process. Post approval, Operational GovOps issues a Reward Code to the Integrator.
        • The Star Agent develops a plan to track USDS utilization attributable to it using either on-chain or off-chain data. Where applicable, the plan should include how the Star Agent will support the prospective Integrator in including the Reward Code in their on-chain infrastructure.

• The Star Agent may also be (or choose to be) an Integrator itself and deploy a Reward Code on its frontend to earn the Accessibility Reward.

    ◦ Near Term process:
        • If the Star Agent is not already an approved Integrator, it must apply to the Program and be approved by Viridian Advisors. Post approval, Viridian Advisors issues a Reward Code to the Star Agent.
        • The Star Agent develops a plan to track USDS utilization attributable to it using either on-chain or off-chain data.

    ◦ Long Term process:
        • The Star Agent must first apply to the Integrator program and be approved by Operational GovOps pursuant to A.2.3.8.1.2.1.1.2.2 - Long Term Process. Post approval, Operational GovOps issues a Reward Code to the Star Agent.
        • The Star Agent develops a plan to track USDS utilization attributable to it using either on-chain or off-chain data.

The required Primitive Inputs for this process are defined herein and organized in sequential stages.

• Drafting of Initial Planning Document

    ◦ Create [code]Initial Planning Document[/code]
        • Updated fields
            • [code]Status[/code]
                ◦ New value: set to [code]Drafting[/code]
            • [code]Integrator[/code]
                ◦ New Value: set to Integrator Name
            • [code]Reward Code[/code]
                ◦ New Value: set to Reward Code
            • [code]Tracking Methodology[/code]
                ◦ New value: populate with details for tracking utilization
            • [code]Custom Instance Parameters[/code]
                ◦ New Value: populate with details for any custom parameters
        • Responsible Party: Star Agent Team
        • Trigger - Required Output: After Star Agent's [code]Initial planning document[/code] Status is set to [code]Drafting[/code] 
• Initial Planning Document Triggered For GovOps review
    ◦ Edit [code]Initial Planning Document[/code]
        • Updated fields
            • [code]Tracking Methodology[/code]
                ◦ New value: updated content, as applicable
            • [code]Status[/code]
                ◦ New value: set to [code]Ready for GovOps Review[/code]
        • Responsible party: Star Agent
        • Trigger - Required Output: After Star Agent's [code]Initial planning document[/code] Status is set to [code]Ready for GovOps review[/code].
The documents herein specify the required outputs from this process.
The Sky Core Atlas is updated pursuant to the following requirements.

A.2.3.8.1.2.1.5.2 - List Of Onboarding Integrators is updated as follows:

• Updated fields
    ◦ [code]Onboarding Integrators/Integrator Name[/code]
        • New value: set to the name of the Integrator
    ◦ [code]Integrator Name/Reward Code[/code]
        • New value: set to Reward Code
• Responsible Party: Operational GovOps

The Agent Artifact is updated pursuant to the following requirements. Each Output “set” is triggered following the completion of its respective Input stage, defined in A.2.3.8.1.2.3.1.3 - Required Primitive Inputs. 

• After Star Agent's [code]Initial planning document[/code] Status is set to [code]Drafting[/code]
    ◦ Create [code]Instance Configuration[/code] Document for prospective Primitive Instance. The Instance Configuration Document contains a [code]Data Repository.[/code]
        • Instance Status: (automatically inherits from [code]Primitive Hub Document[/code])
    ◦ Edit [code]Primitive Hub Document/In Progress Invocations Directory/Instance Name[/code]
        • Updated fields:
            • Invocation Status:
                ◦ New value: set to [code]Planning[/code]
            • Instance Configuration Document Location
                ◦ New value: link to [code]Instance Configuration Document[/code] (created at Create [code]Instance Configuration[/code] Document for prospective Primitive Instance. The Instance Configuration Document contains a [code]Data Repository.[/code])
    ◦ Responsible party: Operational GovOps [automated]
• After Star Agent's [code]Initial planning document[/code] Status is set to [code]Ready for GovOps review[/code].
    ◦ Edit [code]Primitive Hub Document/In Progress Invocations/Instance Name[/code]
        • Updated fields:
            • Invocation Status:
                ◦ New value: set to [code]Pending GovOps review[/code]
        • Responsible party: Operational GovOps [automated]
        • Trigger - Process: A.2.3.8.1.2.3.2 - Process Definition For Operational GovOps Review.

core_children.core_name: Process Definition For Operational GovOps Review
core_children.core_content: The documents herein define the process for Operational GovOps Review for an Invocation of the Accessibility Reward Primitive.
The process initiation logic is specified herein.
Triggers are specified herein.
None.
This process is triggered by the Artifact Document Update specified at Edit [code]Primitive Hub Document/In Progress Invocations/Instance Name[/code].
This process has no dependencies.

The process flow is defined herein:

• Operational GovOps reviews the [code]Initial Planning Document[/code] to ensure:

    ◦ Operational GovOps has the ability to operationalize the proposed tracking mechanism, and

    ◦ The proposed tracking mechanism accurately reflects USDS usage attributable to the actor and there is no possibility that rewards could be “double counted” (i.e. multiple actors being paid for the same USDS balance).

• Operational GovOps submits its feedback into the [code]Operational GovOps Review Document[/code] (created at Create [code]Operational GovOps Review Document[/code].) along with suggested changes, if any.

• The Star Agent incorporates feedback from Operational GovOps and edits its [code]Initial Planning Document[/code] as needed.

The following inputs must be submitted into the Primitive using the Powerhouse interface.

• Create [code]Operational GovOps Review Document[/code].
        •Updated fields
            • [code]Initial Planning Document[/code]
                ◦ New Value: automatically links to respective Document
            • [code]Feedback Summary[/code]
                ◦ New Value: GovOps populates with its review commentary and suggested changes that are agreed to by Agent and GovOps, if any.
            • Responsible party: Operational GovOps.
The documents herein specify the required outputs from this process.
No Sky Core Atlas documents are updated as the output of this process.
The Agent Artifact is updated pursuant to the following requirements.

The Document is updated as follows.

• Updated fields
    ◦ [code]Tracking Methodology[/code]
        • New Value: as applicable, update to reflect any changes agreed to between the Star Agent and Operational GovOps
    ◦ Responsible Party: Operational GovOps

core_children.core_name: Primitive Hub Document/In Progress Invocations Directory/Instance Name  Update
core_children.core_content:
The Document is updated as follows.

• Updated fields
    ◦ Invocation Status
        • New value: set to [code]Proposal drafting in progress[/code]
• Responsible Party: Operational GovOps [automated]
• Triggers: A.2.3.8.1.2.3.3 - Process Definition For Artifact Update Draft

core_children.core_name: Process Definition For Artifact Update Draft
core_children.core_content: The documents herein define the process for preparing the Artifact Update Draft for an Invocation of the Accessibility Reward Primitive.
The process initiation logic is specified herein.
Triggers are specified herein.
None.
This process is triggered by the Document Update specified at A.2.3.8.1.2.3.3.4.2.1 -  Primitive Hub/In Progress Invocations Directory/Instance Name  Update.
This process has no dependencies.

The process flow is defined herein:

• The Star Agent creates the [code]Artifact Edit Draft[/code] Document and works to finalize its draft of the Artifact Edit per discussions with Operational GovOps.

• When draft is finalized, the Star Agent triggers the creation of the [code]Artifact Edit Proposal[/code], which inherits content from the finalized [code]Artifact Edit Draft[/code] Document.

• The Star Agent submits the [code]Artifact Edit Proposal[/code] Document to the Powerhouse system.

The required Primitive Inputs for this process are defined herein and implemented in sequential stages.

• Agent creates [code]Artifact Edit Draft[/code] document; drafting in progress:
    ◦ Edit [code]Artifact Edit Draft[/code]
        • Updated fields
            • Status
                ◦ New value: set to [code]In Progress[/code]
            • Content
                ◦ New value: populate with drafted content

• Agent finalizes [code]Artifact Edit Draft[/code]
    ◦ Edit [code]Artifact Edit Draft[/code]
        • Updated fields
            • Status
                ◦ New value: set to [code]Draft Finalized[/code]

• Powerhouse System Creates [code]Artifact Edit Proposal[/code] Document
    ◦ Updated fields
        • Content
            • New value: Inherits data from Artifact Edit Draft content field.
    ◦ Responsible party: Operational GovOps [if not automated]

• Agent submits [code]Artifact Edit Proposal[/code] Document to Powerhouse system
    ◦ Updated fields
        • Status:
            • New value: set to  [code]Pending Facilitator Review[/code]
    ◦ Responsible party: Agent
The documents herein specify the required outputs from this process.
No Sky Core Atlas documents are updated as the output of this process.
The Agent Artifact documents specified herein are updated as the output of this process.

core_children.core_name: Primitive Hub/In Progress Invocations Directory/Instance Name  Update
core_children.core_content:
The Document is updated as follows:

• Updated fields
    ◦ Invocation Status
        • New value: [code]Proposal Pending Facilitator Review[/code]
• Responsible Party: Operational GovOps
• Triggers: A.2.3.8.1.2.3.4 - Process Definition For Operational Facilitator Review.

core_children.core_name: Process Definition For Operational Facilitator Review
core_children.core_content: The documents herein define the process for Operational Facilitator Review for an Invocation of the Accessibility Reward Primitive.
The process initiation logic is specified herein.
Triggers are specified herein.
None.
This process is triggered by the Document Update specified at A.2.3.8.1.2.3.3.4.2.1 -  Primitive Hub/In Progress Invocations Directory/Instance Name  Update.
This process has no dependencies.

The process flow is defined herein.

• The Operational Facilitator reviews the [code]Artifact Edit Proposal[/code] to ensure alignment with the Sky Core Atlas and the Agent Artifact.

• Where the Proposal is determined to be aligned, the Operational Facilitator updates the [code]Artifact Edit Proposal[/code]  Document to reflect their approval and commentary, if applicable.

• Where the Proposal is determined to be misaligned, the Operational Facilitator updates the [code]Artifact Edit Proposal[/code] document to reflect their rejection and commentary. Commentary is required where the Operational Facilitator rejects the proposal for misalignment.

The required Primitive Inputs to this process are defined herein and organized as two mutually exclusive pathways. Once the Review outcome is determined by the Facilitator, the corresponding Pathway is followed to the exclusion of the other.

• Operational Facilitator Approves Proposal
    ◦ Edit [code]Artifact Edit Proposal[/code]
        • Updated fields
            • [code]Operational Facilitator Review/Review Decision[/code]
                ◦ New value: set to [code]Approved[/code]
            • [code]Commentary[/code]
                ◦ New value (optional): populate with reasoning for Approval
            • [code]Status[/code]
                ◦ New value: set to [code]Proposal Approved by Facilitator[/code] [automated]
        • Responsible party: Operational Facilitator
        • Trigger-Process: A.2.3.8.1.2.3.5 - Process Definition For Offchain Vote 

• Operational Facilitator Rejects proposal
    ◦ Edit [code]Artifact Edit Proposal[/code]
        ◦ Updated fields
            • [code]Operational Facilitator Review/Review Decision[/code]
                ◦ New value: set to [code]Rejected[/code]
            • [code]Commentary[/code]
                ◦ New value (required): populate with reasoning for Rejection
            • [code]Status[/code]
                ◦ New value: set to [code]Proposal Rejected By Facilitator[/code] [automated]
        ◦ Responsible party: Operational Facilitator
The documents herein specify the required outputs from this process, if any.
No Sky Core Atlas documents are updated as the output of this process.
No Agent Artifact documents are updated as the output of this process. The requirements specified in A.2.3.8.1.2.3.4.3 - Required Primitive Inputs fully complete the Process.
The documents herein define the process for an Offchain Vote for an Invocation of the Accessibility Reward Primitive.
The process initiation logic is specified herein.
Triggers are specified herein.
None.
This process is triggered by the Artifact Update specified at Operational Facilitator Approves Proposal.
This process has no dependencies.

The process flow for this process is defined herein:

• Using the finalized [code]Atlas Edit Proposal[/code] content, the Operational Facilitator sets up an offchain Snapshot vote.

• Star Agent token holders vote on the proposal.

• After the voting concludes, the Operational Facilitator records the result of the vote in the Powerhouse system.

The required Primitive Inputs to this process are defined herein.

• After Facilitator prepares Snapshot vote:
    ◦ Edit [code]Artifact Edit Proposal[/code] Document
        • Updated fields
            • Status
                ◦ New value: set to [code]Pending Poll[/code]
            • Off-chain Snapshot
                ◦ New value: Populate with link to the official Snapshot page
        • Responsible party: Operational Facilitator

• Mutually Exclusive Input Pathways: The two Primitive Inputs below are mutually exclusive pathways. Once the vote concludes, the corresponding Pathway is followed to the exclusion of the other.

    ◦ Proposal Passes
        • Edit [code]Artifact Edit Proposal[/code] Document
            • Update fields
                ◦ Status
                    • New value: set to [code]Poll Approved[/code]
            • Responsible party: Operational Facilitator.
            • Trigger - Required Output: Proposal Passes

    ◦ Proposal Fails
        • Updated fields
            • Edit [code]Artifact Edit Proposal[/code] Document
                ◦ Status
                    • New value: set to [code]Poll Rejected[/code]
            • Responsible party: Operational Facilitator.
            • Trigger - Required Output: Proposal Fails
The documents herein specify the required outputs from this process.
No Sky Core Atlas documents are updated as the output of this process.

The Agent Artifact documents specified herein are updated as the output of this process. The Output “sets” are mutually exclusive.

• Proposal Passes

    ◦ Required Primitive Input Trigger: Proposal Passes
    ◦ Edit [code]Primitive Hub Document/In Progress Invocations Directory/Instance Name[/code]
        • Updated fields
            • Invocation Status
                ◦ New value: set to [code]Proposal Approved[/code]
        • Responsible Party: Operational GovOps
        • Trigger - Process: A.2.3.8.1.2.3.6 - Process Definition For Artifact Update 
• Proposal Fails
    ◦ Required Primitive Input Trigger: Proposal Fails
    ◦ Edit [code]Primitive Hub Document/In Progress Invocations Directory/Instance Name[/code]
        • Updated fields
            • Invocation Status
                ◦ New value: set to [code]Proposal Rejected[/code]
        • Other Document Operations:

            • [code]Instance Configuration Document[/code] is [code]Archived[/code] in Primitive Hub Document/Hub Data Repository
        • Responsible Party: Operational GovOps
The documents herein define the Artifact Update process for an Invocation of the Accessibility Reward Primitive.
The process initiation logic is specified herein.
Triggers are specified herein.
None.
This process is triggered by the Artifact update specified in Proposal passes.
This process has no dependencies.

The process flow is defined herein.

• Using the Powerhouse interface, the Operational Facilitator updates the Agent Artifact with the approved Proposal content.

The following inputs must be submitted into the Primitive using the Powerhouse interface.


• Edit [code]Instance Configuration Document[/code] to reflect ratified Primitive Instance.
    ◦ Updated fields
        • Parameters/Status - automatically inherits from [code]Primitive Hub Document[/code]
        • Parameters/Reward Code
            • New Value: set to [code]Reward Code[/code] value from approved Proposal
        • Parameters/Tracking Methodology
            • New Value: set to [code]Tracking Methodology[/code] value from the approved Proposal
        • Parameters/Custom Instance Parameters
            • New Value: set to [code]Custom Instance Parameters[/code] value from approved Proposal
        • Responsible Party: Operational GovOps [if not automated]
The documents herein specify the required outputs from this process.
The Sky Core Documents specified herein are updated as the output of this process.

A.2.3.8.1.2.1.5.2 - List Of Onboarding Integrators is updated as follows:

• Updated fields
    ◦ [code]Onboarding Integrators[/code]
        • New value: Delete the Integrator.
• Responsible Party: Operational Facilitator
• Triggers: None

A.2.3.8.1.2.1.5.1 - List Of Current Integrators is updated as follows:

• Updated fields
    ◦ [code]Current Integrators[/code]
        • New value: set to the name of the Integrator from the approved Proposal
    ◦ [code]Reward Code[/code]
        • New value: set to the Reward Code from the approved Proposal
    ◦ [code]Tracking Methodology[/code]
        • New value: set to the tracking methodology from the approved Proposal
• Responsible Party: Operational Facilitator
• Triggers: None
The Agent Artifact documents specified herein are updated as the output of this process.

The Document is updated as follows:

• Document Operations:
    ◦ Document is moved from [code]In Progress Invocations[/code] to [code]Active Instances[/code]
• Responsible Party: Operational Facilitator

core_children.core_name: Primitive Hub Document/In Progress Invocations Directory/Instance Name  Update
core_children.core_content:
The Document  is updated as follows:

• Document Operations:
    ◦ Document is converted into [code]Active Instances Directory[/code] schema and moved into that subtree
• Updated fields
    ◦ Instance Configuration Document location: link to [code]Instance Configuration Document[/code] in [code]Active Instances[/code] subtree
• Responsible Party: Operational Facilitator
• Trigger-Process: None.
