The documents herein define the process for the ongoing management of an Instance of the Accessibility Reward Primitive.
The documents herein define the protocol for routine ongoing management of an Instance of the Accessibility Reward Primitive.

core_children.core_name: Process Definition For Reward Calculation By Operational Govops
core_children.core_content: The documents herein define the process for Accessibility Reward Calculation by Operational GovOps. The Accessibility Reward Calculation includes the calculation of the Fees for Unrewarded USDS Balances, the Fees for Rewarded USDS Balances, and the Star Agent Management Fee.
The process initiation logic is specified herein.
Triggers are specified herein.
This process is triggered on the 1st of every month for each Instance of the Accessibility Reward Primitive with an Instance Status of [code]Active[/code].
None.
This process has no dependencies.

The process flow is defined herein:

• Operational GovOps calculates the eligible USDS and sUSDS balances using the Tracking Methodology specified in the Primitive Instance.

• Operational GovOps calculates the Accessibility Reward due based on the USDS and sUSDS balances and the Accessibility Reward formula for each.

• Operational GovOps updates the Powerhouse system with both the underlying data and their calculations.

The required Primitive Inputs to this process are specified herein.

• Edit [code]Accessibility Reward Payments[/code] Document (Active Data)
    ◦ Updated fields
        • Status
            • New value: set to [code]In Progress[/code]
        • Underlying data
            • New value: populate with underlying data used to calculate the eligible USDS and sUSDS balances
        • Eligible USDS balance
            • New value: populate with calculated value
        • Eligible sUSDS balance
            • New value: populate with calculated value
        • Accessibility Reward Due
            • New value: populate with calculated value.
    ◦ Responsible party: Operational GovOps.
    ◦ Trigger-Process: A.2.3.8.1.2.4.1.2 - Process Definition For Reward Issuance From Operational Executor Agent Buffer.
The documents herein specify the required outputs from this process.
No Sky Core Atlas documents are updated as the output of this process.
No Agent Artifact documents are updated as the output of this process. The requirements specified A.2.3.8.1.2.4.1.1.3 - Required Primitive Inputs fully complete the Process.

core_children.core_name: Process Definition For Reward Issuance From Operational Executor Agent Buffer
core_children.core_content: The documents herein define the process for Accessibility Reward issuance from the Operational Executor Agent Buffer as part of ongoing management of an Instance of the Accessibility Reward Primitive.
The process initiation logic is specified herein.
Triggers are specified herein.
None.
This process is triggered by the Required Primitive Inputs specified in Edit Accessibility Reward Payments Document (Active Data).
This process has no dependencies.

The process flow is defined herein.

• Operational GovOps makes the payment to the reward address specified in the Primitive Instance from the Operational Executor Agent Buffer.

• Operational GovOps updates the Powerhouse system with the transaction details.

The required Primitive Inputs to this process are specified herein.

• Edit [code]Accessibility Reward Payments[/code] Document (Active Data)
    ◦ Updated fields
        • Status
            • New value: set to [code]Paid[/code]
        • Transaction Details/Amount Paid
            • New value: populate with amount paid
        • Transaction Details/Tx hash
            • New value: populate with transaction hash
    ◦ Responsible Party: Operational GovOps
    ◦ Trigger - Process: A.2.3.8.1.2.4.1.3 - Process Definition For Settlement Cycle And Core GovOps Review.
The documents herein specify the required outputs from this process.
No Sky Core Atlas documents are updated as the output of this process.
No Agent Artifact documents are updated as the output of this process. The requirements specified in A.2.3.8.1.2.4.1.2.3 - Required Primitive Inputs fully complete the process.

core_children.core_name: Process Definition For Settlement Cycle And Core GovOps Review
core_children.core_content: The documents herein define the process for the Accessibility Reward Settlement Cycle and Core GovOps review as part of ongoing management of an Instance of the Accessibility Reward Primitive.
The process initiation logic is specified herein.
Triggers are specified herein.
This process is triggered at the beginning of every calendar quarter.
None.
This process has no dependencies.

The process flow is defined herein.

• Core GovOps reviews Accessibility Rewards calculations, including underlying data and calculation of balances and rewards due.

• Once Core GovOps has completed review, they update Powerhouse system to indicate that they confirm the accuracy of the Accessibility Reward amounts.

The required Primitive Inputs to this process are specified herein and are mutually exclusive pathways.

• Core GovOps Confirms Accuracy of Payment
    ◦ Edit [code]Accessibility Reward Payments[/code] Active Data Document
        • Updated fields
            • Core GovOps Review/Confirmation
                ◦ New value: populate with Yes
            • Core GovOps Review/Commentary
                ◦ New value (optional): populate with reasoning
        • Responsible party: Core GovOps
        • Trigger - Process: A.2.3.8.1.2.4.1.3.4.1 - Sky Core Atlas Updates 

• Core GovOps Finds Inaccurate Payment
    ◦ Edit [code]Accessibility Reward Payments[/code] Active Data Document
        • Updated fields
            • Core GovOps Review/Confirmation
                ◦ New value: populate with No
            • Core GovOps Review/Commentary
                ◦ New value (required): populate with reasoning
        • Responsible party: Core GovOps
        • Trigger - Process: Payment Inaccuracy Previously Found By Core GovOps
The documents herein specify the required outputs from this process.

The Sky Core Atlas documents specified herein are updated as the output of this process.

• Payment Accuracy Previously Confirmed By Core GovOps
    ◦ The document A.2.3.8.1.2.1.6.1 - Sky Core Accessibility Reward Reimbursement Amounts in the Sky Core Atlas is updated as follows:
        • Updated Fields
            • Status
                ◦ New value: populate with [code]Pending Payment[/code]
            • Confirmed Reimbursement Due
                ◦ New value: populate with total Reimbursement amount
            • Reward Period
                ◦ New Value: populate with reward period
            • Operational Executor Agent
                ◦ New value: Populate with name of Operational Executor Agent
            • Star Agent
                ◦ New value: Populate with name of Star Agent.
        • Responsible Party: Core GovOps
        • Triggers: A.2.3.8.1.2.4.1.4 - Process Definition For Executive Vote Reimbursement 
• Payment Inaccuracy Previously Found By Core GovOps
    ◦ TBD
No Agent Artifact documents are updated as the output of this process.

core_children.core_name: Process Definition For Executive Vote Reimbursement
core_children.core_content: The documents herein define the process for Accessibility Reward Executive Vote reimbursement as part of ongoing management of an Instance of the Accessibility Reward Primitive.
The process initiation logic is specified herein.
Triggers are specified herein.
None.
This process is triggered by the Document Update specified in [code]Sky Core Accessibility Reward Reimbursement[/code] Active Data Document Update.
This process has no dependencies.

The process flow is defined herein:

• Core GovOps includes the Accessibility Reward reimbursement in the next standard Executive Vote.

• After the Executive Vote passes, Core GovOps updates the Powerhouse system with the transaction details.

The required Primitive Inputs to this process are specified herein and organized as sequential stages.

• Core GovOps adds reimbursement to Executive Vote
    ◦ Edit [code]Sky Core Accessibility Reward Reimbursement Amounts[/code]
        • Updated fields
            • Executive Vote Settlement/Executive Vote
                ◦ New value: links to proposal
            • Status
                ◦ New value: set to [code]Added to Executive Vote[/code]

• After Executive Vote passes, Core GovOps updates Powerhouse system
    ◦ Edit [code]Sky Core Accessibility Reward Reimbursement Amounts[/code]
        • Updated fields
            • Executive Vote Settlement / Transaction Details/ Amount Paid
                ◦ New value: populate with amount paid to reimburse Operational Executor Agent Buffer
            • Executive Vote Settlement / Transaction Details / Tx Hash
                ◦ New value: Populate with transaction hash
            • Status
                ◦ New value: set to [code]Completed[/code]
The documents herein specify the required outputs from this process.
The Sky Core Atlas documents specified herein are updated as the output of this process.

core_children.core_name: Sky Core Accessibility Reward Reimbursement  Active Data Update
core_children.core_content:
The Document in the Sky Core Atlas is updated as follows:

• Updated fields
    ◦ Status
        • New value: set to [code]Paid[/code]
    ◦ Responsible Party: Core GovOps
    ◦ Trigger-Process: None
No Agent Artifact documents are updated as the output of this process.
The documents herein define the protocol for non-routine ongoing management of an Instance of the Accessibility Reward Primitive.
The documents herein define the protocol for handling emergency situations in the ongoing management of an Instance of the Accessibility Reward Primitive.

section_primary_docs.core_content: The documents herein govern the Integration Boost Primitive.

core_children.core_content: The documents herein provide an introduction to the Integration Boost Primitive.
The Integration Boost Primitive provides a low complexity version of the Sky Savings Rate to integration partners that hold USDS or lending markets that integrate USDS.
Multiple instances of the Integration Boost Primitive are allowed. Each instance corresponds to an Integration Boost program.

An Agent Artifact that has more than one active instance of the Integration Boost Primitive is not required to have a [code]Multi-Instance Coordinator Document[/code], since each Instance can be managed independently.

The requirements herein apply universally across all possible deployments of the Integration Boost Primitive by Star Agents. They include the steps that Agents must take to deploy the Primitive, including Global Activation of the Primitive, Instance Invocation, and ongoing management of the Primitive Instance(s).
The documents herein define the base elements of the Integration Boost Primitive.

The Integration Boost is provided to DeFi protocol partners that allow users to deposit USDS balances. Sky makes payments to Integration Boost partners equal to the Sky Savings Rate times the Unrewarded USDS balances in their protocol. The expectation is that the Integration Boost Partner will pass these Integration Boost Payments along to their users, thus providing USDS users with the equivalent of the Sky Savings Rate.

Integration Boost partners are part of the Integrator Program as defined in A.2.3.8.1.2.1.1 - Integrator Program. 

Current and onboarding Integrators are recorded in A.2.3.8.1.2.1.5 - Current And Onboarding Integrators.
Unrewarded USDS balances are USDS balances that are not receiving the Sky Savings Rate or USDS Token Rewards.

core_children.core_name: No Obligation To Pass Through Integration Boost Payments
core_children.core_content: Integration Boost partners are under no obligation to pass through any portion of Integration Boost payments to their users. Nevertheless, the expectation is that most partners will pass through Integration Boost payments to remain competitive with other protocols.
The documents herein specify data submission requirements related to the Integrator Program.

Integration Boost Partners must submit net deposit data in such a form that the data can be verified by Sky using on-chain data. In the short term, Ecosystem Actor BA Labs calculates the net deposit data based on on-chain events and makes it available to Operational GovOps through an API endpoint. The API endpoint is https://info-sky.blockanalitica.com/api/v1/incentivized-pools/ for Integration Boost Partners on Ethereum and https://info-sky.blockanalitica.com/api/v1/solana-incentives/ on Solana. In the long term, Operational GovOps assumes responsibility for ensuring that the data submission meets the on-chain verifiability requirement; this is done as part of their review of Invocations of the Integration Boost Primitive.

Integration Boost Partners must also identify a responsible party that will submit the net deposit data. In the near term, this is BA Labs. In the long term, this may be the Integration Boost Partner, a third party contractor, or the Star Agent or Operational GovOps themselves if they agree to do so.

Data is calculated on a weekly basis from Monday to Sunday for payment the following Monday. Failure to submit data on time will result in a delay of payment of the Integration Boost until the following week.
The documents herein define base elements of the Integration Boost Primitive related to the distribution of the Integration Boost.
The Cadence is the frequency at which the Integration Boost is calculated and distributed. There are only three options available for the Reward Cadence: (1) weekly, (2) biweekly, or (3) monthly.
The documents herein define the treasury management process.

In the short term, the Support Facilitators directly pay Integration Boost recipients from Integration Boost wallets. The balance of these wallets on the Ethereum Mainnet may be topped up to 3 million USDS, although the balance of Integration Boost wallets across blockchains may exceed that amount. The funding is subject to an Executive Vote. The addresses of these wallets are:

• Ethereum (ETH) - [code]0xd6891d1dffda6b0b1af3524018a1ee2e608785f7[/code]
• Solana (SOL) - [code]7Gf8AqAtmYkkVhQbbJr18RxVUuoGjA8ZEw3Af4NauyaY[/code]
• Solana Hot Wallet - [code]BKT2JR5wRWsXQBxrht1LbbbKtgUkz3sJdaBY8UzgRJPL[/code]

In the long term, Operational GovOps calculates the Integration Boost for each occurrence of the specified Cadence. Operational GovOps then pays the Integration Boost recipient from its Buffer. Later, Sky Core reimburses the Operational Agent Buffer for the amount paid as part of the Settlement Cycle. This minimizes the role of Sky Core in Integration Boost payments and emphasizes the primary role of the Operational Executor Agent, acting through Operational GovOps, in implementing the Sky Primitives. The process is specified in further detail in A.2.3.8.2.2.4.1 - Routine Protocol.

If it is discovered that previous Integration Boost calculations were made erroneously, underpayments are resolved retroactively. In cases where an Integrator was overpaid, the Star Agent associated with the Integrator must reimburse Sky the overpayment amount and can use future Integration Boost payments that the Integrator earns to reimburse itself.
Net USDS balances in a DeFi protocol that is receiving an Integration Boost are also eligible for the Accessibility Reward.

core_children.core_name: Reporting Of Net USDS Balances Is Valid Tracking Methodology
core_children.core_content: The methodology used to report net USDS balances in the protocol for the Integration Boost is itself an acceptable Tracking Methodology for purposes of the Accessibility Reward.
Accessibility Reward may only be paid on net USDS balances of an Integration Boost partner to the extent that an Accessibility Reward is not already being paid on those balances.

core_children.core_name: Star Agent May Choose Whether To Share Accessibility Reward With Integration Boost Partner
core_children.core_content: Star Agents may choose whether to share Accessibility Reward payments with Integration Boost Partners.
An Agent who intends to deploy the Integration Boost Primitive must first Globally Activate it.
The process initiation logic is specified herein.
Triggers are specified herein.
None.
None.
See A.2.3.1.2.4 -  Changing A Primitive’s Global Activation Status for constraints on when an Agent can Globally Activate this Primitive.
The Star Agent uses the Powerhouse interface to Globally Activate (toggle on) the Integration Boost Primitive.

The following inputs must be submitted into the Primitive using the Powerhouse interface:

• Create [code]Primitive Hub Document[/code]
    ◦ Updated Field: Global Activation Status
        • New Value: set to [code]Activated[/code]
The documents herein specify the required outputs from this process.
No Sky Core Atlas documents are updated as the output of this process.
No Agent Artifact documents are updated as the output of this process. The requirements specified in A.2.3.8.2.2.2.3 - Required Primitive Inputs fully complete the Process.

After fulfilling the requirements for Global Activation, an Agent can Invoke its first Instance of the Integration Boost Primitive by following the sequential process specified herein. Subsequent Invocations of the Primitive must adhere to the same requirements defined below.

core_children.core_name: Process Definition For Initial Opportunity Identification And Planning
core_children.core_content: The documents herein specify the process definition for initial opportunity identification and planning.
The process initiation logic is specified herein.
Triggers are specified herein.
None.
None.
This process is dependent on a Star Agent Globally Activating the Integration Boost Primitive pursuant to A.2.3.8.2.2.2 - Global Activation.

The process flow is defined herein.

• The Star Agent identifies a DeFi protocol or market where an Integration Boost would drive adoption. The Star Agent estimates the potential earnings from the Accessibility Reward associated with the incremental USDS usage versus the operational cost of funding the Sky Savings Rate payouts.

• The Star Agent and the prospective Integration Boost partner discuss (1) the proposed Integration Boost cadence and (2) whether and how the protocol operator also receives a portion of the Accessibility Reward.

The required Primitive Inputs for this process are defined herein and organized in sequential stages.

• Drafting of Initial Planning Document

    ◦ Create [code]Initial Planning Document[/code]
        • Updated fields
            • [code]Status[/code]
                ◦ New value: set to [code]Drafting[/code]
            • [code]Integration Partner Name[/code]
                ◦ New Value: set to Integration Partner name
            • [code]Integration Partner Reward Address[/code]
                ◦ New Value: set to Integration Partner reward address
            • [code]Integration Partner Chain[/code]
                ◦ New Value: set to Integration Partner chain
            • [code]Integration Boost Cadence[/code]
                ◦ New Value: set to Integration Boost cadence
            • [code]Integration Boost Data Submission Format[/code]
                ◦ New Value: populate with details for format of data submission
            • [code]Integration Boost Data Submission Responsible Actor[/code]
                ◦ New Value: set to Actor responsible for data submission
            • [code]Integration Boost Savings Rate Adjustment Strategy[/code]
                ◦ New Value: populate with details for handling adjustments to Sky Savings Rate
            • [code]Custom Instance Parameters[/code]
                ◦ New Value: populate with details for any custom parameters
        • Responsible Party: Star Agent Team
        • Trigger - Required Output: After Star Agent's [code]Initial planning document[/code] Status is set to [code]Drafting[/code] 

• Initial Planning Document triggered for GovOps review

    ◦ Edit [code]Initial Planning Document[/code]
        • Updated fields
            • [code]Integration Boost Savings Rate Adjustment Strategy[/code]
                ◦ New value: updated content, as applicable
            • [code]Status[/code]
                ◦ New value: set to [code]Ready for GovOps Review[/code]
        • Responsible party: Star Agent
        • Trigger - Required Output: After Star Agent's [code]Initial planning document[/code] Status is set to [code]Ready for GovOps review[/code].
The documents herein specify the required outputs from this process.
The Sky Core Atlas is updated pursuant to the following requirements.

A.2.3.8.1.2.1.5.2 - List Of Onboarding Integrators is updated as follows:

• Updated fields
    ◦ [code]Onboarding Integrators/Integrator Name[/code]
        • New value: set to the name of the Integrator
• Responsible Party: Operational GovOps

The Agent Artifact is updated pursuant to the following requirements. Each Output “set” is triggered following the completion of its respective Input stage, which latter is defined in A.2.3.8.2.2.3.1.3 - Required Primitive Inputs. 

• After Star Agent's [code]Initial planning document[/code] Status is set to [code]Drafting[/code]
    ◦ Create [code]Instance Configuration[/code] Document for prospective Primitive Instance. The Instance Configuration Document contains a [code]Data Repository[/code].
        • Instance Status: (automatically inherits from [code]Primitive Hub Document[/code])
    ◦ Edit [code]Primitive Hub Document/In Progress Invocations Directory/Instance Name[/code]
        • Updated fields:
            • Invocation Status:
                ◦ New value: set to [code]Planning[/code]
            • Instance Configuration Document Location
                ◦ New value: link to [code]Instance Configuration Document[/code] (created at Create [code]Instance Configuration[/code] Document for prospective Primitive Instance. The Instance Configuration Document contains a [code]Data Repository[/code].)
    ◦ Responsible party: Operational GovOps [automated]

• After Star Agent's [code]Initial planning document[/code] Status is set to [code]Ready for GovOps review[/code].
    ◦ Edit [code]Primitive Hub Document/In Progress Invocations/Instance Name[/code]
        • Updated fields:
            • Invocation Status:
                ◦ New value: set to [code]Pending GovOps review[/code]
        • Responsible party: Operational GovOps [ automated]
        • Trigger - Process: A.2.3.8.2.2.3.2 - Process Definition for Operational GovOps Review.

core_children.core_name: Process Definition for Operational GovOps Review
core_children.core_content: The documents herein define the process for Operational GovOps Review for an Invocation of the Integration Boost Primitive.
The process initiation logic is specified herein.
Triggers are specified herein.
None.
This process is triggered by the Artifact Update specified at Edit [code]Primitive Hub Document/In Progress Invocations/Instance Name[/code].
This process has no dependencies.

The process flow is defined herein:

• Operational GovOps reviews the [code]Initial Planning Document[/code] to ensure:
    ◦ The submitted data can be verified using on-chain data;
    ◦ the Savings Rate Adjustment Strategy ensures that the payment on USDS balances equals the Sky Savings Rate, and
    ◦ the submitted data accurately reflects USDS deposits in the Integration Partner protocol and there is no possibility that rewards could be “double counted” (i.e. multiple actors being paid for the same USDS balance).

• Operational GovOps submits its feedback into the [code]Operational GovOps Review Document[/code] (created at Create [code]Operational GovOps Review Document[/code]) along with suggested changes, if any.

• The Star Agent incorporates feedback from Operational GovOps and edits its [code]Initial Planning Document[/code] as needed.

The following inputs must be submitted into the Primitive using the Powerhouse interface.

• Create [code]Operational GovOps Review Document[/code]
    ◦ Updated fields
        • [code]Initial Planning Document[/code]
            • New Value: automatically links to respective Document
        • [code]Feedback Summary[/code]
            • New Value: GovOps populates with its review commentary and suggested changes that are agreed to by Agent and GovOps, if any.
        • Responsible party: Operational GovOps.
The documents herein specify the required outputs from this process.
No Sky Core Atlas documents are updated as the output of this process.
The Agent Artifact is updated pursuant to the following requirements.

The Document is updated as follows.

• Updated fields
    ◦ [code]Integration Boost Savings Rate Adjustment Strategy[/code]
        • New Value: as applicable, update to reflect any changes agreed to between the Star Agent and Operational GovOps
    ◦ [code]Integration Boost Data Submission Format[/code]
        • New Value: as applicable, update to reflect any changes agreed to between the Star Agent and Operational GovOps
    ◦ Responsible Party: Operational GovOps

The Document is updated as follows.

• Updated fields
    ◦ Active instances/Instance name/Instance status -
        • New value: set to [code]Proposal drafting in progress[/code]
• Responsible Party: Operational GovOps [automated]
• Triggers: A.2.3.8.2.2.3.3 - Process Definition for Artifact Update Draft.

core_children.core_name: Process Definition for Artifact Update Draft
core_children.core_content: The documents herein define the process for preparing the Artifact Update Draft for an Invocation of the Integration Boost Primitive.
The process initiation logic is specified herein.
Triggers are specified herein.
None.
This process is triggered by the Document Update specified at A.2.3.8.2.2.3.2.4.2.2 - Primitive Hub Document Update.
This process has no dependencies.

The process flow is defined herein:

• The Star Agent creates the [code]Artifact Edit Draft[/code] Document and works to finalize its draft of the Artifact Edit per discussions with Operational GovOps.

• When draft is finalized, the Star Agent triggers the creation of the [code]Artifact Edit Proposal[/code], which inherits content from the finalized [code]Artifact Edit Draft[/code] Document.

• The Star Agent submits the [code]Artifact Edit Proposal[/code] Document to the Powerhouse system.

The required Primitive Inputs for this process are defined herein and implemented in sequential stages.

• Agent creates [code]Artifact Edit Draft[/code] document; drafting in progress

    ◦ Edit [code]Artifact Edit Draft[/code]
        • Updated fields
            • Status
                ◦ New value: set to [code]In Progress[/code]
            • Content
                ◦ New value: populate with drafted content

• Agent finalizes [code]Artifact Edit Draft[/code]
    ◦ Edit [code]Artifact Edit Draft[/code]
        • Updated fields
            • Status
                ◦ New value: set to [code]Draft Finalized[/code]

• Powerhouse System Creates [code]Artifact Edit Proposal[/code] Document
    ◦ Updated fields
        • Content
            • New value: Inherits data from [code]Artifact Edit Draft[/code] Document’s Content field.
    ◦ Responsible party: Operational GovOps [if not automated]

• Agent submits [code]Artifact Edit Proposal[/code] Document to Powerhouse system
    ◦ Updated fields
        • Status:
            • New value: set to  [code]Pending Facilitator Review[/code]
    ◦ Responsible Party: Agent
The documents herein specify the required outputs from this process.
No Sky Core Atlas documents are updated as the output of this process.
The Agent Artifact documents specified herein are updated as the output of this process.

The Document in the Agent Artifact is updated as follows:

• Updated fields
    ◦ Active Instances/Instance Name/Instance Status
        • New value: [code]Proposal Pending Facilitator Review[/code]
• Responsible Party: Operational GovOps
• Triggers: A.2.3.8.2.2.3.4 - Process Definition for Operational Facilitator Review.

core_children.core_name: Process Definition for Operational Facilitator Review
core_children.core_content: The documents herein define the process for Operational Facilitator Review for an Invocation of the Integration Boost Primitive.
The process initiation logic is specified herein.
Triggers are specified herein.
None.
This process is triggered by the Document Update specified at A.2.3.8.2.2.3.2.4.2.2 - Primitive Hub Document Update.
This process has no dependencies.

The process flow is defined herein.

• The Operational Facilitator reviews the [code]Artifact Edit Proposal[/code] to ensure alignment with the Sky Core Atlas and the Agent Artifact.

• Where the Proposal is determined to be aligned, the Operational Facilitator updates the [code]Artifact Edit Proposal[/code]  Document to reflect their approval and commentary, if any.

• Where the Proposal is determined to be misaligned, the Operational Facilitator updates the [code]Artifact Edit Proposal[/code] document to reflect their rejection and commentary. Commentary is required where the Operational Facilitator rejects the proposal for misalignment.

The required Primitive Inputs to this process are defined herein and organized as two mutually exclusive pathways. Once the Review outcome is determined by the Facilitator, the corresponding Pathway is followed to the exclusion of the other.

• Operational Facilitator Approves Proposal
    ◦ Edit [code]Artifact Edit Proposal[/code]
        • Updated fields
            • [code]Operational Facilitator Review/Review Decision[/code]
                ◦ New value: set to [code]Approved[/code]
            • [code]Commentary[/code]
                ◦ New value (optional): populate with reasoning for Approval
            • [code]Status[/code]
                ◦ New value: set to [code]Proposal Approved by Facilitator[/code] [automated]
        • Responsible party: Operational Facilitator
        • Trigger-Process: A.2.3.8.2.2.3.5 - Process Definition for Offchain Vote.
 
• Operational Facilitator Rejects Proposal
    ◦ Edit [code]Artifact Edit Proposal[/code]
        • Updated fields
            • [code]Operational Facilitator Review/Review Decision[/code]
                ◦ New value: set to [code]Rejected[/code]
            • [code]Commentary[/code]
                ◦ New value (required): populate with reasoning for Rejection
            • [code]Status[/code]
                ◦ New value: set to [code]Proposal Rejected By Facilitator[/code] [automated]
        • Responsible party: Operational Facilitator
The documents herein specify the required outputs from this process, if any.
No Sky Core Atlas documents are updated as the output of this process.
No Agent Artifact documents are updated as the output of this process. The requirements specified in A.2.3.8.2.2.3.4.3 - Required Primitive Inputs fully complete the Process.
The documents herein define the process for an Offchain Vote for an Invocation of the Integration Boost Primitive.
The process initiation logic is specified herein.
Triggers are specified herein.
None.
This process is triggered by the Artifact Update specified at Operational Facilitator Approves Proposal.
This process has no dependencies.

The process flow for this process is defined herein:

• Using the finalized [code]Atlas Edit Proposal[/code] content, the Operational Facilitator sets up an offchain Snapshot vote.

• Star Agent token holders vote on the proposal.

• After the voting concludes, the Operational Facilitator records the result of the vote in the Powerhouse system.

The required Primitive Inputs to this process are defined herein.

• After Facilitator prepares Snapshot vote:
    ◦ Edit [code]Artifact Edit Proposal[/code] Document
        • Updated fields
            • Status
                ◦ New value: set to [code]Pending Poll[/code]
            • Off-chain Snapshot
                ◦ New value: Populate with link to the official Snapshot page
        • Responsible party: Operational Facilitator

• Mutually Exclusive Input Pathways: The two Primitive Inputs below are mutually exclusive pathways. Once the vote concludes, the corresponding Pathway is followed to the exclusion of the other.

    ◦ Proposal Passes
        • Edit [code]Artifact Edit Proposal[/code] Document
            • Update fields
                ◦ Status
                    • New value: set to [code]Poll Approved[/code]
            • Responsible party: Operational Facilitator.
            • Trigger - Required Output: Proposal passes

    ◦ Proposal Fails
        • Updated fields
            • Edit [code]Artifact Edit Proposal[/code] Document
                ◦ Status
                    • New value: set to [code]Poll Rejected[/code]
            • Responsible party: Operational Facilitator.
            • Trigger - Required Output: Proposal fails
The documents herein specify the required outputs from this process.
No Sky Core Atlas documents are updated as the output of this process.

The Agent Artifact documents specified herein are updated as the output of this process. The Output “sets” are mutually exclusive. 

• Proposal passes
    ◦ Required Primitive Input Trigger: Proposal Passes 
    ◦ Edit [code]Primitive Hub Document[/code]
        • Fields Updated
            • Active instances/instance name/instance status - set to [code]Approved[/code]
        • Responsible Party: Operational GovOps
        • Trigger - Process: A.2.3.8.2.2.3.6 - Process Definition for Artifact Update. 

• Proposal fails
    ◦ Required Primitive Input Trigger: Proposal Fails 
    ◦ Edit [code]Primitive Hub Document[/code]
        • Fields Updated
            • Active instances/instance name/instance status - set to [code]Rejected[/code]
        • Other Document Operations:
            • [code]Instance Configuration Document[/code] is [code]Archived[/code] in Primitive Hub Document/Data Repository
        • Responsible Party: Operational GovOps
The documents herein define the Artifact Update process for an Invocation of the Integration Boost Primitive.
The process initiation logic is specified herein.
Triggers are specified herein.
None.
This process is triggered by the Artifact update specified in Proposal passes.
This process has no dependencies.

The process flow is defined herein.

• Using the Powerhouse interface, the Operational Facilitator updates the Agent Artifact with the approved Proposal content.

The following inputs must be submitted into the Primitive using the Powerhouse interface.

• Edit [code]Instance Configuration Document[/code] to reflect ratified Primitive Instance.
    ◦ Updated fields
        • Parameters/Status - automatically inherits from [code]Primitive Hub Document[/code]
        • Parameters/Integration Partner Name
            • New Value: set to [code]Integration Partner Name[/code] value from approved Proposal
        • Parameters/Integration Partner Reward Address
            • New Value: set to [code]Integration Partner Reward Address[/code] value from approved Proposal
        • Parameters/Integration Partner Chain
            • New Value: set to [code]Integration Partner Chain[/code] value from approved Proposal
        • Parameters/Integration Boost Cadence
            • New Value: set to [code]Integration Boost Cadence[/code] value from approved Proposal
        • Parameters/Integration Boost Data Submission Format
            • New Value: set to [code]Integration Boost Data Submission Format[/code] value from approved Proposal
        • Parameters/Integration Boost Data Submission Responsible Actor
            • New Value: set to [code]Integration Boost Data Submission Responsible Actor[/code] value from approved Proposal
        • Parameters/Integration Boost Savings Rate Adjustment Strategy
            • New Value: set to [code]Integration Boost Savings Rate Adjustment Strategy[/code] value from approved Proposal
        • Parameters/Custom Instance Parameters
            • New Value: set to [code]Custom Instance Parameters[/code] value from approved Proposal
        •  Responsible party: Operational GovOps [if not automated]
The documents herein specify the required outputs from this process.
The Sky Core Documents specified herein are updated as the output of this process.

A.2.3.8.1.2.1.5.2 - List Of Onboarding Integrators is updated as follows:

• Updated fields
    ◦ [code]Onboarding Integrators[/code]
        • New value: Delete the Integrator.
• Responsible Party: Operational Facilitator
• Triggers: None

A.2.3.8.1.2.1.5.1 - List Of Current Integrators is updated as follows:

• Updated fields
    ◦ [code]Current Integrators[/code]
        • New value: set to the name of the Integrator from the approved Proposal
• Responsible Party: Operational Facilitator
• Triggers: None
The Agent Artifact documents specified herein are updated as the output of this process.

The Document in the Agent Artifact is updated as follows:

• Required Primitive Input Trigger: Proposal passes
• Updated fields
    ◦ Active Instances/Instance Name/Instance Status
        • New value: [code]Active[/code]
    ◦ Instance Location: [links to [code]Instance Configuration Document[/code] subtree]
• Responsible Party: Operational Facilitator
• Trigger-Process: None.
The documents herein define the process for the ongoing management of an Instance of the Integration Boost Primitive.
The documents herein define the routine process for ongoing management of an Instance of the Integration Boost Primitive.

core_children.core_name: Process Definition For Integration Boost Calculation By Operational GovOps
core_children.core_content: The documents herein define the process for Integration Boost Calculation by Operational GovOps.
The process initiation logic is specified herein.
Triggers are specified herein.
This process is triggered at the end of each occurrence of the Cadence for each Instance of the Integration Boost Primitive with an Instance Status of [code]Active[/code].
None.
This process has no dependencies.

The process flow is defined herein:

• At the end of each Integration Boost period, the data provider submits the net USDS deposit data to Powerhouse.

• Operational GovOps verifies this data by checking on-chain balances and confirms that there are no discrepancies.

• Operational GovOps then calculates the payout based on the net USDS deposit data and the Sky Savings Rate.

The required Primitive Inputs to this process are specified herein.

• Edit [code]Integration Boost Payments[/code] Document (Active Data)
    ◦ Updated fields
        • Status
            • New value: set to [code]In Progress[/code]
        • Underlying data
            • New value: populate with underlying data used to calculate the net deposits
        • Net deposits
            • New value: populate with calculated value
        • Integration Boost payment due
            • New value: populate with calculated value.
    ◦ Responsible party: Operational GovOps.
    ◦ Trigger-Process: A.2.3.8.2.2.4.1.2 - Process Definition For Integration Boost Payment Issuance From Operational Executor Agent Buffer.
The documents herein specify the required outputs from this process.
No Sky Core Atlas documents are updated as the output of this process.
No Agent Artifact documents are updated as the output of this process. The requirements specified A.2.3.8.2.2.4.1.1.3 - Required Primitive Inputs fully complete the Process.

core_children.core_name: Process Definition For Integration Boost Payment Issuance From Operational Executor Agent Buffer
core_children.core_content: The documents herein define the process for Integration Boost payment from the Operational Executor Agent Buffer.
The process initiation logic is specified herein.
Triggers are specified herein.
None.
This process is triggered by the Required Primitive Inputs specified in Edit Integration Boost Payments Document (Active Data).
This process has no dependencies.

The process flow is defined herein.

• Operational GovOps makes the payment to the reward address specified in the Primitive Instance from the Operational Executor Agent Buffer.

• Operational GovOps updates the Powerhouse system with the transaction details.

The required Primitive Inputs to this process are specified herein.

• Edit [code]Integration Boost Payments[/code] Document (Active Data)
    ◦ Updated fields
        •  Status
            • New value: set to [code]Paid[/code]
        • Transaction Details/Amount Paid
            • New value: populate with amount paid
        • Transaction Details/Tx hash
            • New value: populate with transaction hash
    ◦ Responsible Party: Operational GovOps
    ◦ Trigger - Process: A.2.3.8.2.2.4.1.3 - Process Definition For Settlement Cycle And Core GovOps Review.
The documents herein specify the required outputs from this process.
No Sky Core Atlas documents are updated as the output of this process.
No Agent Artifact documents are updated as the output of this process. The requirements specified in A.2.3.8.2.2.4.1.2.3 - Required Primitive Inputs fully complete the process.

core_children.core_name: Process Definition For Settlement Cycle And Core GovOps Review
core_children.core_content: The documents herein define the process for the Integration Boost Settlement Cycle and Core GovOps review as part of ongoing management of an Instance of the Integration Boost Primitive.
The process initiation logic is specified herein.
Triggers are specified herein.
This process is triggered at the beginning of every calendar quarter.
None.
This process has no dependencies.

The process flow is defined herein.

• Core GovOps reviews Integration Boost calculations, including underlying data and calculation of balances and rewards due.

• Once Core GovOps has completed review, they update Powerhouse system to indicate that they confirm the accuracy of the Integration Boost amounts.

The required Primitive Inputs to this process are specified herein.

• Edit [code]Integration Boost Payments[/code] Active Data Document
    ◦ Updated field
        • Core GovOps Review/Confirmation
            • New value: populate with yes or no.
        • Core GovOps Review/Commentary
            • New value: populate with reasoning (required if confirmation value is [code]No[/code])
    ◦ Responsible party: Core GovOps
The documents herein specify the required outputs from this process.
The Sky Core Atlas documents specified herein are updated as the output of this process.

core_children.core_name: Sky Core Integration Boost Reimbursement  Active Data Document Update
core_children.core_content:
The document in the Sky Core Atlas is updated as follows:

• Updated Fields
    ◦ Status
        • New value: populate with [code]Pending Payment[/code]
    ◦ Confirmed Reimbursement Due
        • New value: populate with total Reimbursement amount
    ◦ Reward Period
        • New Value: populate with reward period
    ◦ Operational Executor Agent
        • New value: Populate with name of Operational Executor Agent
    ◦ Star Agent
        • New value: Populate with name of Star Agent.
• Responsible Party: Core GovOps
• Triggers: A.2.3.8.2.2.4.1.4 - Process Definition For Executive Vote Reimbursement.
No Agent Artifact documents are updated as the output of this process.

core_children.core_name: Process Definition For Executive Vote Reimbursement
core_children.core_content: The documents herein define the process for Integration Boost Executive Vote reimbursement as part of ongoing management of an Instance of the Integration Boost Primitive.
The process initiation logic is specified herein.
Triggers are specified herein.
None.
This process is triggered by the Document Update specified in A.2.3.8.2.2.4.1.3.4.1.1 -  Sky Core Integration Boost Reimbursement  Active Data Document Update.
This process has no dependencies.

The process flow is defined herein:

• Core GovOps includes the Integration Boost reimbursement in the next standard Executive Vote.

• After the Executive Vote passes, Core GovOps updates the Powerhouse system with the transaction details.

The required Primitive Inputs to this process are specified herein and organized as sequential stages.

• Core GovOps adds reimbursement to Executive Vote
    ◦ Edit [code]Sky Core Integration Boost Reimbursement Amounts[/code] 
        •  Updated fields
            • Executive Vote Settlement/Executive Vote
                ◦ New value: links to proposal
            • Status
                ◦ New value: set to [code]Added to Executive Vote[/code]

• After Executive Vote passes, Core GovOps updates Powerhouse system
    ◦ Edit [code]Sky Core Integration Boost Reimbursement Amounts[/code]
        •  Updated fields
            • Executive Vote Settlement / Transaction Details/ Amount Paid
                ◦ New value: populate with amount paid to reimburse Operational Executor Agent Buffer.
            • Executive Vote Settlement / Transaction Details / Tx Hash
                ◦ New value: Populate with transaction hash
            • Status
                ◦ New value: set to [code]Completed[/code]
The documents herein specify the required outputs from this process.
The Sky Core Atlas documents specified herein are updated as the output of this process.

core_children.core_name: Sky Core Integration Boost Reimbursement Active Data Update
core_children.core_content:
The Document in the Sky Core Atlas is updated as follows:

• Updated fields
    ◦ Status
        • New value: set to [code]Paid[/code]
    ◦ Responsible Party: Core GovOps
    ◦ Trigger-Process: None
No Agent Artifact documents are updated as the output of this process.
The documents herein define processes for handling non-routine situations in the ongoing management of an Instance of the Integration Boost Primitive.
The documents herein define processes for handling emergency situations in the ongoing management of an Instance of the Integration Boost Primitive.

section_primary_docs.core_content: The documents herein govern the Pioneer Chain Primitive.

core_children.core_content: The documents herein provide an introduction to the Pioneer Chain Primitive.

Pioneer Stars must be designated by the official team or foundation of the Pioneer Chain, they must be created for the specific purpose of being a Pioneer Star from their genesis moment, and it is only possible for a Pioneer Chain to have Pioneer Star once, and for a Pioneer Star to have the Pioneer Star status with a single blockchain, once.

Pioneer Stars gain benefits and responsibilities related to the general adoption of USDS on the Pioneer Chain, and this benefit comes in two forms. 

First, during the Pioneer Phase, the Pioneer Star counts as having tagged, for the purposes of calculating the Accessibility Reward, all USDS accounts and balances on the Pioneer Chain that have not been tagged by another Star. At the end of the Pioneer Phase, all untagged USDS accounts and balances are one-time tagged by the Pioneer Star, and this tag will remain normally for the following ten (10) years unless tagged by a different Star, or retagged.

Second, during the Pioneer Phase, all USDS that is bridged to the Pioneer Chain, and is not getting the Sky Savings Rate, either through sUSDS, Integration Boost, or by being held by an Agent, counts as unrewarded USDS.

Every monthly settlement cycle, an amount of funds equivalent to the Sky Savings Rate applied to the unrewarded USDS is paid into the Pioneer Star’s SubProxy Account (the “Pioneer Incentive Pool”). 80% of the funds going to the Pioneer Incentive Pool are not the property of the Pioneer Stars and must instead be used as incentives to promote general adoption of USDS on the Pioneer Chain. This must provide general benefit for the sake of growing USDS activity on the Pioneer Chain and cannot be biased towards benefitting the Pioneer Star. The remaining 20% of the funds going to the Pioneer Incentive Pool may be retained by the Pioneer Star as income.

article_sections.section_content: Supply Side Stablecoin Primitives are Sky Primitives focused on capital allocation and risk management.

section_primary_docs.core_content: The Allocation System Primitive is the base mechanism that enables Star Agents to put up Risk Capital to deploy USDS collateral into different opportunities that provide risk-adjusted return, by borrowing at the Base Rate from Sky, and following Asset Liability Management restrictions on the liquidity of deployed assets.

core_children.core_content: The documents herein define the process for initial setup and ongoing management of an Allocation Instance (alternatively referred to as “conduits”) as part of the Allocation System Primitive.
The documents herein define the process for setting up an Allocation Instance as part of the Allocation System Primitive.
The documents herein define the preliminary, off-chain human coordination stage of setting up an Allocation Instance.

The Star Agent identifies a yield opportunity on a target chain that can exceed the Base Rate. The Star Agent estimates Required Risk Capital (RRC) and Asset Liability Management requirements associated with the opportunity and evaluates options for fulfilling them. The Star Agent also considers exchange and bridging costs, if applicable. Based on this, the Star Agent develops a net yield target for the opportunity factoring in all of these costs. The output of this step is an internal meeting note summarizing the idea as well as estimates of Required Risk Capital, Asset Liability Management, and exchange / bridging costs.

core_children.core_name: Preliminary Required Risk Capital and Asset Liability Management Arrangement
core_children.core_content: If necessary, the Star Agent reaches out to other Star Agents that could provide Junior Risk Capital (JRC) / Senior Risk Capital (SRC) or assume Asset Liability Management obligations. The Star Agent negotiates with these other Star Agents and documents these potential deals. The output of this step is an informal confirmation of JRC/SRC and Asset Liability Management transfer details.

core_children.core_name: Preliminary Informal Check With Operational GovOps
core_children.core_content: The Star Agent presents the Capital and Operational Plan for the Allocation Instance to Operational GovOps, including any associated JRC/SRC and Asset Liability Management arrangements. Operational GovOps reviews and either provides an informal greenlight or requests modifications to the Plan. The output of this step is Operational GovOps approval to proceed with a request to revise the C&O Plan, as applicable.
The documents herein define how agreements to set up an Allocation Instance are codified and validated in the Powerhouse system and how governance votes happen.

The Star Agent drafts an update to the Star Agent Artifact adding the Allocation Instance to the list of active Allocation Instances and including the information specified in A.2.3.9.1.2.6 - List Of Allocation Instances. The Star Agent submits the draft to the Powerhouse system. The output of this step is a draft Star Agent Artifact update in the Powerhouse system.

The Operational Executor Facilitator reviews the proposal to ensure that it is complete and aligned with the Atlas. The Operational Executor Facilitator then initiates an off-chain snapshot vote, following the quorum and majority rules in the Star Agent Artifact. When complete, the result of the vote is recorded in the Powerhouse system. The output of this step is the snapshot vote result recorded in the Powerhouse system.

If the Allocation Instance is successfully approved, the Operational Executor Facilitator finalizes and publishes the update to the Star Agent Artifact, making it effective in the Atlas. The output of this step is an updated Star Agent Artifact with the new Allocation Instance. 

Once a new Instance is deployed, its data is integrated into risk-capital monitoring systems and its RRC will be officially determined and tracked via the Sky Core RRC Dashboard/API. See A.2.3.9.1.1.2.2.1.1 -  Sky Core Required Risk Capital (RRC) Dashboard And API. This official RRC figure supersedes the Star-prepared pro-forma RRC.
The documents herein define how the deployment of an Allocation Instance is executed on-chain.

Once formally validated, the Star Agent implements the new Allocation Instance based on the specifications in the Star Agent Artifact. Operational GovOps must test the Allocation Instance with minimal funds from the Star Agent’s Central Allocation Buffer, verifying deposit / withdrawal logic, slippage constraints, and oracle checks. If this testing is successful Operational GovOps confirms that the Allocation Instance satisfies all risk constraints specified in the Star Agent Artifact. The output of this step is validated contracts for the Allocation Instance and a green light to proceed with a scaled deployment.

core_children.core_name: Initial Deployment and Required Risk Capital / Asset Liability Management Execution
core_children.core_content: The Star Agent finalizes JRC/SRC and Asset Liability Management Arrangements. Operational GovOps records these arrangements in the Powerhouse interface. If Operational GovOps is charged with operationalizing an Instance, it deploys funds from the Star Agent’s Operational Buffer into the Allocation Instance, performing any necessary DEX exchanges. See A.2.3.9.1.1.2.1 -  Operationalization Of Allocation Instances. In doing so, Operational GovOps follows instructions in the Agent Artifact regarding the amount of capital to deploy and slippage tolerances. The output of this step is a fully deployed Allocation Instance conforming to Risk Capital and Asset Liability Management requirements.
The documents herein define the process for managing an Allocation Instance as part of the Allocation System Primitive.

core_children.core_name: Allocation Conduit Management Periodic Data Submission And Validation
core_children.core_content: At specified intervals, data providers submit yield, exchange rate, First Loss Capital, and Asset Liability Management data as specified in the data submission protocol for the Allocation Conduit in the Star Agent Artifact. Operational GovOps cross-checks the data submitted against on-chain and off-chain for consistency. If the Allocation Conduit is significantly underperforming or there are violations of First Loss Capital or Asset Liability Management requirements, Operational GovOps must initiate escalation steps as defined in the Star Agent Artifact.

core_children.core_name: Allocation Instance Adjustments, Scaling, And Settlement
core_children.core_content: On an ongoing basis, the Star Agent team or Operational GovOps operates the Allocation Instance, scaling exposure up and down. Any change to the Allocation Instance outside of the limits specified in the Star Agent Artifact or requiring judgment on the part of Operational GovOps (where the Allocation System Instance is operationalized by the Operational Executor Agent) requires a new vote of Star Agent token holders. See A.2.3.9.1.1.2.3.1 -  Modification Of Existing Instances. As part of monthly and quarterly settlement cycles, Core GovOps reviews yields and obligations, applying penalties retroactively for any previously undisclosed violations. In the event of any such violations, Operational GovOps may be required to take escalatory steps based on the fallback strategy in the Star Agent Artifact.

The documents herein define the required inputs for a valid Invocation of the Allocation System Primitive. If any input is noncompliant or omitted, the Primitive will be invalidated and the Allocation Instance deployment will not move forward.
The Allocation System Primitive must be Globally Activated.
The Star Agent Artifact must specify the address of the Star Agent’s Core Allocation Vault.
The Star Agent Artifact must specify the address of the Star Agent’s Core Allocation Buffer.
The Star Agent Artifact must specify the rate limiters for the Allocation Vault and Core Allocation Buffer, including the address and parameters for each.
The Star Agent Artifact must specify the address of the Star Agent’s Capital Faucet and its rate limits.

If the Allocation System Primitive is Activated, then the Star Agent Artifact must list each active Allocation Instance, grouped by blockchain. For each blockchain, the Star Agent Artifact must specify: (1) the name of the blockchain, (2) the bridging mechanism for the blockchain, if any, (3) the allocation buffer on the blockchain, and (4) the Allocation Instances on the blockchain. For each Allocation Instance, the Star Agent Artifact must contain the information specified herein.

For each Allocation Instance, the Star Agent Artifact must specify: (1) the name of the Allocation Instance, (2) the address of the Allocation Instance, (3) the rate limit of the conduit for inflows and outflows, (4) any other technical parameters of the Allocation Instance (e.g. maximum slippage, oracles used), (5) data submission protocols, and (6) specification of emergency measures that can be used by Operational GovOps if Required Risk Capital or Asset Liability Management obligations are breached.

For each Allocation Instance, the Star Agent Artifact must specify the strategy documents contained herein. These strategy documents must describe in detail how the Allocation Instance should be operated so that the Operational Executor Agent that the Star Agent has contracted with can operate the Allocation Instance without further input from the Star Agent or having to make “judgment calls”. To the extent that the Star Agent’s strategy cuts across different Allocation Instances, the Star Agent may include a single set of strategy documents covering all Allocation Instances.

core_children.core_name: Required Allocation Instance Asset Liability Management Strategy
core_children.core_content: The allocation strategy for each Allocation Instance should specify how Operational GovOps should adjust the amount of funds allocated to the Conduit based on performance, market conditions, or other factors. It should also specify limits to the amount by which the allocation can be adjusted before a vote of Star Agent token holders is required.

core_children.core_name: Required Allocation Conduit Asset Liability Management Strategy
core_children.core_content: The asset liability management strategy for each Allocation Instance must specify what steps Operational GovOps should take to ensure that the Star Agent continues to satisfy its Asset Liability Management obligations as the amount of funds allocated to the Conduit changes.

The fallback strategy for each Allocation Instance must specify how Operational GovOps should determine when an emergency situation is occurring regarding the Allocation Instance and what actions it should take to protect the Star Agent from losses associated with the Allocation Instance. The fallback strategy must include emergency measures for Operational GovOps to reduce exposure to, drain, and ultimately shut down the Allocation Instance if Required Risk Capital or Asset Liability Management obligations are breached.

section_primary_docs.core_content: The Junior Risk Capital Rental Primitive is a mechanism enabling Star Agents to rapidly rent Junior Risk Capital from each other, ensuring that capital gets deployed to where the best opportunities are.

The Actively Stabilizing Collateral Rental Primitive is a mechanism enabling Star Agents to trade Asset Liability Management obligations between each other, providing more flexibility in how capital is deployed through the Allocation System and reducing duplicate work.

article_sections.section_content: Core Governance Primitives allow Star Agents to earn incentives for maintaining and securing Sky Governance frontends as well as borrow from the Smart Burn Engine.

section_primary_docs.core_content: The Core Governance Reward Primitive is a reward that Sky pays to Star Agents that provide SKY holders with secure access to the core Sky Governance features, ensuring that the Governance Security of Sky is maintained over time.

scope_articles.article_content: The Sky Treasury Management Function defines how all Net Revenue of the Sky Protocol is distributed among various downstream functions or buffers. This ensures that all necessary functions are adequately funded, allowing all actors to focus on growing the ecosystem in a positive sum way.

article_sections.section_content: The documents herein define the Sky Treasury Management Function.

section_primary_docs.core_content: The Sky Treasury Management Function is synchronized with the Monthly Settlement Cycle. See A.2.5 - Sky Core Monthly Settlement Cycle. At the conclusion of each MSC, the Net Revenue of the Sky Protocol for the preceding month is calculated and allocated according to the waterfall process defined in A.2.4.1.2 - Allocation Steps.

The documents herein define the allocation steps of the Sky Treasury Management Function. The process is divided into steps beginning with Net Revenue. These steps form a “waterfall” with each previous step needing to be fully funded before any funds can be allocated to later steps.

core_children.core_content: The sole function of Step 0 is to establish the Net Revenue of the Sky Protocol. It performs no allocations to downstream functions or buffers, unlike subsequent steps for which this Net Revenue serves as the input.

Net Revenue is equal to Income minus Expenses. Income is defined in A.2.4.1.2.1.2 - Income. Expenses are defined in A.2.4.1.2.1.3 - Expenses. Income and Expenses are defined such that Net Revenue must always be positive
The documents herein define each of the components of the Income of the Sky Protocol. These components are added together to arrive at total Income.

Stability Fees are the fees that Sky Core charges Star Agents to borrow from Sky. See A.3.2.2.1 - Base Rate. Sky Core’s legacy ALM infrastructure is currently being transferred to Star Agents; during this transition period and until this transfer is fully complete, income generated from the Sky Core Collateral Portfolio contributes to net revenue. See A.3.4.1.4 - Application To Sky Core.

Internal Senior Risk Capital income is the revenue attributed to Sky from the total payments made by Stars for Originated Senior Risk Capital (OSRC). ISRC itself comprises surplus capital allocated from designated buffers funded by the Sky Treasury Management Function. While Stars pay a single clearing price for their OSRC based on the monthly Origination Process, the total revenue received by Sky is subsequently allocated proportionally, with the portion corresponding to ISRC's share of the Total Senior Risk Capital (TSRC) pool designated as ISRC Income. See A.2.4.1.3 - Sourcing Of Internal Senior Risk Capital   From Surplus Buffers.

External Senior Risk Capital Fees are levied by Sky and calculated as 5% of the net interest earnings generated by the External Senior Risk Capital (ESRC) pool each Monthly Settlement Cycle. These earnings represent the portion of the total revenue from Stars' Originated Senior Risk Capital (OSRC) payments that is attributed proportionally to ESRC, based on its contribution to the Total Senior Risk Capital (TSRC) pool for that cycle. This 5% fee is deducted before the remaining ESRC earnings contribute to the srUSDS conversion rate. See A.3.3.2.4.2.3.3.2 - ESRC Earnings Fee.
Agent Upkeep Fees are fees paid by Agents to contribute to the long-term sustainability of the Sky Ecosystem. See A.2.3.6 -  Ecosystem Upkeep Primitives.
Agent Creation Fees are one-time payments collected from founding teams when establishing new Star Agents. See A.2.3.3.1.2 -  Creation Fee.
The documents herein define each of the components of Expenses. These components are added together to arrive at total Expenses.

core_children.core_name: Sky Savings Rate Paid To sUSDS Holders
core_children.core_content: The Sky Savings Rate is the interest expense paid to sUSDS holders on their balances. It also includes interest paid to sDai holders on their balances.

core_children.core_name: Sky Savings Rate Paid Through Integration Boost
core_children.core_content: The Integration Boost provides the equivalent of the Sky Savings Rate to users of decentralized finance protocols who hold USDS balances. See A.2.3.8.2 - Integration Boost Primitive.
The Accessibility Reward is paid to Star Agents and third-party partners that drive adoption of USDS. See A.2.3.8.1 - Accessibility Reward Primitive.
Reimbursement Rewards are paid to Star Agents to reimburse them for the costs of building common infrastructure used by the entire Sky Ecosystem, such as Token SkyLink deployments.
Pioneer Rewards are paid to Pioneer Stars as specified in A.2.3.8.3.1.3 - Pioneer Incentive Pool.

All of the Net Revenue from Step 0 (A.2.4.1.2.1.1 - Net Revenue) becomes Step 1 Capital. 10% of Step 1 Capital is allocated for security and stability maintenance as specified in the documents herein. The remaining 90% of Step 1 Capital becomes Step 2 Capital and is allocated as specified in A.2.4.1.2.3 - Step 2: High Activity Staking Rewards.

Up to 5% of Step 1 Capital is allocated to the Core Executor Budget. This budget funds active Core Executor Agents responsible for the continuous improvement of the Atlas, including the risk management framework and other critical components.

core_children.core_name: Voluntary Reduction By Core Executor Agents
core_children.core_content: Core Executor Agents may agree to voluntarily reduce their budget below 5% when applying for a Core Executor Slot in order to increase their chances of being elected to a Core Executor Slot. To the extent that Core Executor Agents reduce their budget, Step 2 Capital is increased by a corresponding amount. The mechanism by which Core Executor Agents are elected will be specified in a future iteration of the Atlas.

Up to 3% of Step 1 Capital is allocated to Core Executors whose terms have ended in the last four (4) years. This incentivizes Core Executors to increase the long term profitability of the Sky Ecosystem during their Core Executor Slot terms, as the Core Executor Reward is a percentage of future Net Revenue.
Up to 1% of Step 1 Capital is allocated to Aligned Delegates.

Aligned Delegates may agree to voluntarily reduce their compensation in order to incentivize SKY holders to delegate to them. To the extent that Aligned Delegates reduce their compensation, Step 2 Capital is increased by a corresponding amount. The mechanism by which Aligned Delegates may agree to reduce their compensation will be specified in a future iteration of the Atlas.

core_children.core_name: Governance Accessibility Rewards Paid To Integrators
core_children.core_content: 0.5% of Step 1 Capital is allocated to frontends and other Integrators that facilitate accessible governance, following security and information standards specified in the Atlas. These standards will be specified in a future iteration of the Atlas. See A.2.3.10.1 -  Core Governance Reward Primitive.

core_children.core_name: Governance Accessibility Rewards Paid To Star Agents
core_children.core_content: 0.5% of Step 1 Capital is allocated to Star Agents that manage the rewards for Governance Integrators, incentivizing the active participation of Star Agents. See A.2.4.1.2.2.4 - Governance Accessibility Rewards Paid To Integrators.
5% of the Step 2 Capital is allocated to High Activity Staking Rewards (”HASR”) as specified in the documents herein. The remaining 95% of Step 2 Capital becomes Step 3 Capital.

High Activity Staking Rewards provide a boosted staking reward to SKY stakers that delegate their SKY through compliant Governance Accessibility frontends and provide feedback to their delegates every three (3) months. This ensures that there is a minimum amount of SKY that is always actively following current events and governance to detect potential misalignment or buildup of hidden risks.

core_children.core_name: Funding Of High Activity Staking Rewards Buffer
core_children.core_content: 5% of the Step 2 Capital is allocated to funding the High Activity Staking Rewards Buffer (HASRB) linearly over the course of the month. Funds from the High Activity Staking Rewards Buffer are then paid out to Governance Accessibility Integrators as specified in A.2.4.1.2.3.3 - Payout From High Activity Staking Rewards Buffer. Governance Accessibility Integrators are in turn responsible for distributing those funds to High Activity Stakers as specified in A.2.4.1.2.3.4 - High Activity Staker Qualification.

core_children.core_name: Payout From High Activity Staking Rewards Buffer
core_children.core_content:
The High Activity Staking Rewards distribution occurs linearly over the course of each month. The total amount distributed equals the lesser of 1) 5% of Step 2 Capital, or 2) 15% of the current High Activity Staking Rewards Buffer, with a minimum floor of 5% of HASRB. 

In periods of high profitability, funds build up in the High Activity Staking Rewards Buffer. Then in periods of low profitability these funds are paid out from the High Activity Staking Rewards Buffer, ensuring that there is a continued incentive for active involvement in governance even in periods of low profitability to maintain security.
To qualify as High Activity Stakers, SKY stakers must satisfy two criteria as specified in the documents herein.

core_children.core_name: High Activity Stakers Must Stake Through Compliant Governance Accessibility Frontend
core_children.core_content: High Activity Stakers must stake through Governance Accessibility frontends that comply with security and information standards specified in the Atlas.

core_children.core_name: High Activity Stakers Must Continuously Provide Input On Executor Agents
core_children.core_content: High Activity Stakers must continuously provide input on the work of Executor Agents. In this way High Activity Stakers monitor the performance of Executor Agents just as Executor Agents monitor the performance of Star Agents.

The methods for High Activity Stakers to provide their input will be specified in a future iteration of the Atlas, but may for instance include filling out AI generated questionnaires or other easily measurable and easy to complete tasks. The Core Executor Agents must continuously improve these input methods.
A percentage of the Step 3 Capital equal to the Stability Capital Retention (”SCR”) is allocated to funding the Stability Capital Buffer. The remaining percentage becomes Step 4 Capital.

The Stability Capital Buffer is the surplus of the Sky Protocol that is able to be the final absorber of losses in the case of unsuccessful liquidation of failed Star Agents. See A.3.6.1 - Surplus Buffer.

The Stability Capital Retention ([code]SCR[/code]) is calculated according to the following formula:

[code]SCR = (1 - ((SCB / TS) / TSCB)) * RF[/code].

Where:
◦ SCB = Current Stability Capital Buffer,
◦ TS = Total USDS supply,
◦ TSCB = Target Stability Capital Buffer,
◦ RF = Retention Factor.

This formula dynamically adjusts capital retention based on the current buffer status:
1. When SCB/TS equals TSCB (buffer at target), SCR equals zero.
2. When SCB/TS is below TSCB, SCR increases proportionally to the shortfall.
3. The maximum possible retention is capped by the Retention Factor (RF).

This ensures that capital retention automatically scales with urgency - the further the Stability Capital Buffer falls below its target percentage of total supply, the greater the portion of capital retained.

The parameters for calculating the [code]SCR[/code] are further defined in the documents herein.
The Current Stability Capital Buffer ([code]SCB[/code]) is the current level of the Stability Capital Buffer.
The total USDS Supply ([code]TS[/code]) is the total supply of USDS.

The Target Stability Capital Buffer ([code]TSCB[/code]) is the Target level of the Stability Capital Buffer, expressed as a percentage of the total supply of USDS. It is calculated according to the following formula:

[code]TCSB = 0.75% + (TS * 1.4e-14)[/code].

The [code]TSCB[/code] cannot exceed 5%.

This formula begins with a Target Stability Capital Buffer of 0.75% and gradually increases this as the Total USDS Supply increases, reflecting the fact that a greater capital buffer is required as USDS becomes more systemically important.

For example, with USDS Supply of 8 billion, the Target Stability Capital Buffer would be approximately 0.76%.

The Retention Factor is a factor representing how aggressively capital should be retained to fund the Stability Capital Buffer. It is calculated according to the following formula:

[code]RF = 25% + (TS * 1.6e-13)[/code].

The [code]RF[/code] cannot exceed 75%.

The Retention Factor starts at 25% and scales upward with Total USDS Supply, recognizing that capital accumulation becomes more critical as USDS gains in systemic importance.

For example, with USDS Supply of 8 billion, the Retention Factor would be approximately 26%.

core_children.core_name: Step 4: Smart Burn And Standard Activity Staking Rewards
core_children.core_content: Any remaining funds in Step 4 Capital are allocated as specified in the documents herein.
80% of Step 4 Capital is allocated to the Smart Burn Buffer, as specified in the documents herein.

The Smart Burn Engine uses the Smart Burn Buffer to buy and burn SKY using an intelligent algorithm, buying more when the price is low and retaining capital when the price is high to buy later at favorable prices.

The Smart Burn Engine only buys and burns SKY when the Market Capitalization ([code]MC[/code]) is below the Target Market Capitalization ([code]TMC[/code]). The percentage of the Smart Burn Buffer ([code]Burn Rate[/code]) that is used to buy and burn SKY each Monthly Settlement Cycle is calculated according to the following formula:

[code]Burn Rate = (1 - MC / TMC) * 50%[/code].

The parameters for calculating the [code]Burn Rate[/code] are defined in the documents herein.

Conceptually, this formula buys more SKY the further the Market Capitalization is below the Target Market Capitalization.

The Target Market Capitalization ([code]TMC[/code]) is calculated according to the following formula:

[code]TMC = 8.5 + (200 * growth_rate) * annual_profits[/code].

The [code]growth_rate[/code] is the three (3) year moving average historical growth rate of Net Revenue, and [code]annual_profits[/code] is the one (1) year moving average of Net Revenue.

Conceptually, this formula calculates a target price based on the current earnings of the Sky Protocol and a price to earnings ratio, where the price to earnings ratio is a function of how quickly the Sky Protocol is growing.
The Core Executor Agents must continuously improve the Target Market Capitalization formula so that the Smart Burn Engine becomes more efficient over time.

The Market Capitalization is the current market capitalization of Sky. It is calculated by multiplying the price of SKY by the total supply of SKY, treating each MKR on an as-converted basis based on the conversion ratio of 24,000 SKY to 1 MKR.
20% of Step 4 Capital is allocated to the Standard Activity Staking Rewards Buffer, as specified in the documents herein.
Standard Activity Staking Rewards (SASR) provide rewards to users who contribute to security by staking and delegating their SKY votes at least once.

core_children.core_name: Payout From Standard Activity Staking Rewards Buffer
core_children.core_content:
The Standard Activity Staking Reward Distribution occurs linearly over the course of each month. The monthly payout from the Standard Activity Staking Rewards Buffer (SASRB) is equal to the lesser of (1) 20% of Step 4 Capital, or (2) 15% of the SASRB, with a minimum floor of 5% of the SASRB.

In periods of high profitability, funds build up in the Standard Activity Staking Rewards Buffer. Then in periods of low profitability these funds are paid out from the Standard Activity Staking Rewards Buffer, ensuring that there is a continued incentive for involvement in governance even in periods of low profitability to maintain security.
SKY that is both staked and delegated to an Aligned Delegate will qualify for Standard Activity Staking Rewards.

section_primary_docs.core_name: Sourcing Of Internal Senior Risk Capital   From Surplus Buffers
section_primary_docs.core_content: Internal Senior Risk Capital (ISRC) consists of a portion of the excess capital of the Sky Protocol that is reinvested in providing Senior Risk Capital to Star Agents. ISRC is sourced from the various Buffers defined in A.2.4.1.2 - Allocation Steps, according to the specifications of the subdocuments below.

core_children.core_name: USDS in High Activity Staking Rewards Buffer
core_children.core_content: All USDS in the High Activity Staking Rewards Buffer is used for Internal Senior Risk Capital.

core_children.core_name: USDS In Standard Activity Staking Rewards Buffer
core_children.core_content: All USDS in the Standard Activity Staking Rewards Buffer is used for Internal Senior Risk Capital.
All USDS in the Smart Burn Buffer is used for Internal Senior Risk Capital.

core_children.core_name: USDS In Stability Capital Buffer Above Target
core_children.core_content: All USDS in the Stability Capital Buffer above the Stability Capital Buffer Target is used for Internal Senior Risk Capital. Since this capital is already above the optimal target to absorb risk it can be redirected to a more efficient use.

core_children.core_name: USDS In Stability Capital Buffer Below Target
core_children.core_content: One third of the USDS in the Stability Capital Buffer below the Stability Capital Buffer Target is also used for Internal Senior Risk Capital. This ensures that there is always some Internal Senior Risk Capital to drive the growth of the ecosystem.

section_primary_docs.core_content: As a short-term transitionary measure, the net revenue of the Sky Protocol is currently allocated manually through Executive Votes rather than through the allocation steps defined above. This manual process will be removed in a future iteration of the Atlas.

scope_articles.article_content: This Article governs the Sky Core Monthly Settlement Cycle (MSC), a recurring process that synchronizes core financial operations, governance functions, and risk management activities across the ecosystem.

article_sections.section_content: The documents herein define the Monthly Settlement Cycle (MSC), a standardized, system-wide process executed at the end of each calendar month.

section_primary_docs.core_content:
The Monthly Settlement Cycle (MSC) synchronizes several key operational processes across the ecosystem, including:
    
1. Sky Protocol’s net revenue from the previous month is calculated and allocated through the steps of the Treasury Management Function. See A.2.4 - Treasury Management. 
   
2. The monthly Senior Risk Capital (SRC) origination process is settled: the clearing price is established, costs are deducted from winning Star Agents’ accounts, and their accounts are credited with Originated SRC (OSRC) for the upcoming month. See A.3.3.2.4.3.5 - Settlement Of Origination. 
    
3. Queued conversions between USDS and srUSDS within the SRC system are processed. See A.3.3.2.4.2.2 - Deposit And Redemption Queues. 
    
4. Pioneer Incentive Pools are funded with an amount equivalent to the Sky Savings Rate applied to unrewarded USDS. See A.2.3.8.3 - Pioneer Chain Primitive. 
    
5. The Smart Burn Buffer's burning operation occurs synchronously with each Monthly Settlement Cycle.
